value.Title: Southern Company Stock: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish?

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-V233-S1VG-H1MM-00000-00Southern Company Stock: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish?2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisUTILITIES NEWS & EQUITY MARKETS NEWSSouthern Company Stock: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish?Neharika Jain

Valued at a market cap of $108.2 billion, The Southern Company (SO) is an integrated energy provider, delivering a comprehensive suite of utility and infrastructure solutions. The Atlanta, Georgia-based company generates, transmits, and distributes reliable electricity and natural gas, supported by a massive fleet that includes industry-leading nuclear capacity, modern natural gas facilities, and a rapidly expanding renewable energy portfolio. 

This utility company has underperformed the broader market over the past 52 weeks. Shares of SO have gained 5.3% over this time frame, while the broader S&P 500 Index ($SPX) has rallied 28.5%. However, on a YTD basis, the stock is up 10%, outpacing SPX's 6% rise.

More Top Stocks Daily:

Go behind Wall Street's hottest headlines with Barchart's Active Investor newsletter.

 

Narrowing the focus, SO has also lagged the State Street Utilities Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLU), which soared 16.6% over the past 52 weeks. Nonetheless, it has outpaced XLU's 8.6% YTD rise. 

On Apr. 30, shares of SO soared 3.4% after it delivered better-than-expected Q1 earnings results. Due to higher electric and natural gas revenue, the company's total operating revenue increased 8% year-over-year to $8.4 billion, surpassing consensus estimates by 3.8%. Moreover, its adjusted EPS of $1.32 grew 7.3% from the same period last year, handily topping analyst expectations of $1.21. 

For the current fiscal year, ending in December, analysts expect SO's EPS to grow 6.3% year over year to $4.57. The company's earnings surprise history is mixed. It topped the consensus estimates in three of the last four quarters, while missing on another occasion.  

Among the 25 analysts covering the stock, the consensus rating is a "Moderate Buy," which is based on six "Strong Buy," one "Moderate Buy," 17 "Hold," and one "Strong Sell" rating.  

The configuration is slightly less bullish than a month ago, with seven analysts suggesting a "Strong Buy" rating.    

On May 4, Argus Research maintained a "Buy" rating on SO, with a price target of $101, indicating a 5.3% potential upside from the current levels.    

The mean price target of $103.03 suggests a 7.4% premium to its current levels, while its Street-high price target of $114 implies an 18.9% potential upside from the current price levels. 

On the date of publication, Neharika Jain did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policy here.

value.Title: IKEA GROUP - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G2PC-00000-00IKEA GROUP - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA GROUP ANNOUNCES NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN, GA

IKEA Group has announced the opening of its new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia. This location is set to open its doors on the 22nd of April, 2026, and will feature a grand opening celebration at 11 a.m. with exclusive tours, giveaways, food sampling, and more.Located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, the new IKEA Newnan is designed to enhance customer accessibility to the brand. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, expressed enthusiasm for the opening, stating, "The Newnan location is just one of the many ways we are expanding our business. We are excited to open our doors and eagerly anticipate welcoming Newnan area residents to this new IKEA experience." The Plan & Order Point provides a more tailored shopping experience. Unlike traditional large-format IKEA stores, this location allows customers to meet with the IKEA team to plan and order home furnishing solutions that require additional assistance, such as kitchens and bathroom layouts. Although customers cannot take products home on the same day, IKEA staff can arrange delivery or facilitate free pick-ups from the convenient onsite location.

+ IKEA GROUP ACCELERATES AI DRIVEN OMNICHANNEL TRANSFORMATION

IKEA Group is accelerating its transition to an omni-channel retail model powered by artificial intelligence. The company says roughly 30 percent of its business now comes from digital channels. On the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, outlined how AI investments are set to reshape both customer experiences and back end operations.The plan features a Bengaluru digital hub that signals a commitment to innovation beyond local operations. India is a dynamic app driven market, and the hub will support local projects as well as global initiatives. The recent acquisition of Locus aims to strengthen last mile delivery and reinforce IKEA's omni-channel approach in the region.AI is also transforming internal processes. AI based design tools have shortened room layout planning from hours to minutes. IKEA Kreativ, an AI driven platform, enables customers to visualize how products fit into their homes and offers several design options at different price levels. Taken together these digital efforts underpin sustainable growth and ethical AI practices as IKEA expands in India and other markets.

+ IKEA FINLAND LAUNCHES DISABILITY-FOCUSED PILOT AT VANTAA STORE

IKEA Group has launched an accessibility pilot at its Vantaa store in Finland, aiming to improve the shopping experience for customers with disabilities. The program began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping services with app-based navigation to bridge digital planning and in-store purchases. Two core features are being tested: a pre-booked 90-minute assisted-shopping session with a trained colleague providing personalized support, and the NaviLens app, which lets customers scan store codes to receive audio guidance on product areas and departmental locations. Both options are free and let shoppers choose their level of help. "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs," says Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland. The company says the pilot is a learning platform to gather feedback and refine the services for possible rollout elsewhere, as part of an ongoing push to create inclusive journeys online and in-store. The effort is supported by IKEA's Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise, and the broader context includes the World Health Organization estimate that about 1.3 billion people globally live with a significant disability.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) LEVERAGES DATA AND INFLUENCERS TO BOOST CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is redefining its marketing approach by merging technology, data analytics and social media influence to drive customer engagement and sales. On 6th May 2026, Molly Battin, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) chief marketing officer, shared insights about the company’s innovative strategies that extend beyond traditional advertising. Battin notes that the shopping journey often begins with urgent home-improvement needs. By using first-party data and regional weather signals, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) tailors product recommendations and messages in real time, ensuring customers receive relevant information at critical moments. The Home Depot app is central to this approach, with app users spending three to four times more than non-users, a trend driven by younger shoppers who favor guided experiences through the app over in-person conversations with sales staff. Features like Store Mode help customers navigate the retailer’s large warehouses more efficiently. The Home Depot is also expanding its creator economy through a creator portal to cultivate partnerships with influencers who connect with DIY enthusiasts and professionals. Battin emphasizes quality over quantity, focusing on creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values. Early campaigns have driven new customer acquisition in niche categories, illustrating how CMOs are becoming more technology-enabled and financially accountable.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) ANALYSTS LIFT PRICE TARGETS ON PRO GROWTH MOMENTUM

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, analysts raised price targets for the company into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS setting a new target at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism tied to the Q4 results and renewed PRO focus. Guggenheim and Wells Fargo cited meaningful progress in Home Depot's PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings on its long-term strategy and ongoing investmentsHowever, some analysts remained cautious on the near-term outlook. RBC Capital and Bernstein held lower targets with neutral views, citing subdued home improvement demand and macro factors that could constrain growth. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets modestly, signaling unease about remodeling demand's impact on revenueIn addition to its revised fiscal 2026 guidance calling for total sales growth of about 2.5% to 4.5%, Home Depot unveiled PRO enhancements, including an expanded Pro digital workspace for project planning and real-time deliveries, and a forthcoming real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value estimate stands at US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions around 4.19% and adjustments to profit margins and future P/E multiples as investors assess the PRO initiatives and guidance

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) POSTS DEBUT IN SAN MARCOS WITH $13 MILLION STORE PROJECT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) Posts Debut in San Marcos With a $13 Million Store Project, Confirmed on 28th April 2026 The project marks the retailer's first location in this rapidly growing college town, which has seen an influx of residents and retail opportunities in recent years. Construction plans have been filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR), and the footprint totals 135,918 square feet including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction slated to begin in September 2026 and finish in August 2027. The site sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and expected to open in October 2026. San Marcos lies on the I-35 corridor beside Texas State University, fueling growth in housing, retail and infrastructure. The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) also reported a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations across the United States, reflecting its ongoing expansion in active markets

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PARTNERS WITH MADE IN TO EXCLUSIVELY LAUNCH CAST IRON COLLECTION

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) has teamed with Made In to launch an exclusive cast iron collection that will be sold only through Williams-Sonoma stores starting 11th May 2026, with the line hitting Made In's direct-to-consumer site by June 1st, in time for Father's Day. Williams-Sonoma will also stock Made In's American-made stainless steel collection, broadening the brand's premium kitchenware lineup. Made In, founded in 2017, serves more than 2,000 restaurants and has seen revenue grow 52% from 2024 to 2025, with a sizable portion of sales in the fourth quarter. The new cast iron line has been refined to be lighter while maintaining performance, addressing consumer interest in non toxic materials and longevity. The partnership followed years of discussions with Williams-Sonoma, aimed at delivering a differentiated product. In addition to Williams-Sonoma, Made In's products are sold at Crate & Barrel, Bloomingdale's and Ace Hardware, and the company plans to establish a European fulfillment center by September. The cast iron launch is a key focus for Made In this year as it works to position itself as a leading provider of premium cookware.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) RECEIVES BUY RATING FROM GOLDMAN SACHS

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a positive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.Based on the projections, Williams-Sonoma is expected to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates. However, non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast to be $13.00, down 33.82% from earlier projections.This upgrade aligns with a broader reassessment of the home goods sector as consumers adjust spending patterns. Williams-Sonoma, a leading player in home furnishings, could benefit from ongoing home-improvement and decor investments as the new financial year approaches.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PROMOTES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO LEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined Williams-Sonoma in 2003 as a district manager for Pottery Barn and has since risen through the Rejuvenation banner, most recently serving as senior vice president since 2020.In her new role, Taylor will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives designed to position Rejuvenation as a stronger pillar within the Williams-Sonoma portfolio. CEO Laura Alber said Taylor’s leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to become a billion-dollar brand, noting her track record, operational excellence, and passion for design.Rejuvenation has expanded its retail footprint with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has pursued collaborations such as a lighting collection with Roll & Hill. Williams-Sonoma has also named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The company continues to expect 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE AND RESIDENT HOME AGREE TO $9 MILLION CLASS-ACTION SETTLEMENT OVER FIBERGLASS MATTRESS CLAIMS

Ashley Furniture Industries LLC and Resident Home LLC are set to distribute $9 million in a class action settlement over certain mattresses sold between October 1, 2017 and June 30, 2024. The suit alleges these mattresses contained fiberglass as a fire retardant material that could escape and pose harm to consumers, with vouchers offered to eligible buyers. Eligible customers are those who purchased affected mattresses designed, manufactured, or sold by the two companies during the period. The settlement provides vouchers on a pro rata basis funded by the settlement amount, with any remaining funds potentially issued as cash payments to eligible claimants. Claimants must file by July 17, 2026 to receive a voucher usable on purchases at AshleyFurniture.com or NectarSleep.com. Documentation is required; those who received an email with a unique ID and PIN may complete only part of the form, while others must provide proof of purchase such as receipts, store records, or photos of mattress labels. The final approval hearing is scheduled for September 24, 2026, after which vouchers will be distributed once any appeals are resolved, and the companies have not admitted wrongdoing.

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE EXPANDS INTO UPSCALE SEGMENT WITH LUXE COLLECTION

Ashley Furniture Industries is expanding into the upscale segment with the Luxe collection, unveiled May 1, 2026. The move marks a strategic shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores worldwide.Luxe covers living, dining, and bedroom furniture and will be available in about 70% of Ashley's locations, through company-owned outlets, licensees, and online. Sofas run between $1,000 and $1,200, and sectionals range from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, says Luxe broadens Ashley's reach and differentiates the brand in a crowded market. The line relies on Ashley's integrated supply chain, combining domestic manufacturing with imports to keep prices competitive.Dedicated Luxe sections and signage will appear in stores, with the Las Vegas flagship featuring the line prominently. A marketing push featuring Hailee Steinfeld supports the rollout, while CEO Todd Wanek frames Luxe as an evolution that remains within reach.

+ AMAZON EXPANDS NORTHWEST FOOTPRINT WITH $200 MILLION SPANAWAY DISTRIBUTION COMPLEX ACQUISITION

Amazon has acquired Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—a 1.1 million square-foot facility and surrounding parcels—for more than $200 million, a deal reported on the 27th of April 2026.The transaction fits Amazon's ongoing effort to streamline its supply chain and bolster warehousing capacity, a priority as e-commerce grows and customers demand faster deliveries in the Pacific Northwest.Ashley Furniture Industries, a major player in home furnishings, sold the Spanaway property as part of a strategic realignment. The sale may indicate a shift in the company's distribution focus, with implications for its future logistics strategy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS PRO FINANCING WITH MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the launch of the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card, developed with Synchrony, to serve professional contractors and extend payment acceptance beyond in-store purchases, following an announcement on the 6th of May 2026. Built to align with Lowe's contractor-focused strategy, the card works anywhere American Express is accepted and aims to deepen relationships with pro buyers across daily business transactions while supporting loyalty and checkout flexibility. Lowe's sets long-term targets in its investor plan, projecting $100.6 billion in revenue and $8.1 billion in earnings by 2029, with expected annual revenue growth of 5.3 percent and earnings rising from $6.6 billion to $8.1 billion. Analysts note the near-term earnings impact may be limited as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides margin discipline amid a flat to low single-digit comparable sales outlook for 2026

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) POSTS FIVE NEW STORE OPENINGS LED BY PORT ST. LUCIE

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts Five New Store Openings Led by Port St. LucieLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space, a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, and will employ more than 100 associates. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms designed to blend style, affordability and innovation. New Lowe's showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Beyond Port St. Lucie, Lowe's has four other openings planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. This expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet the rising demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. Redman noted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is built to deliver value, convenience and the service the community expects from Lowe's. Investors will watch how these locations contribute to overall sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS U.S. FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORE OPENINGS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) expands its U.S. footprint with new store openings, including a Florida location slated for early June 2026 and four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky expected to begin operations later this year. The Port St. Lucie store will span about 94,000 square feet and feature a 30,000-square-foot garden center, employing more than 100 staff members, and will incorporate redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup.Lowe's currently operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, handling roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales surpassed $83 billion. The retailer also emphasizes its MyLowe's Rewards program, which offers points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, supporting loyalty and repeat business, while continuing community initiatives aimed at safe, affordable housing and workforce development

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) BROADENS HEMP-DERIVED THC BEVERAGE DISTRIBUTION AMID REGULATORY AMBIGUITY

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is expanding its hemp-derived THC beverage distribution pilot beyond Minnesota to Florida, Illinois and Texas, with the rollout beginning May 11, 2026, and remaining subject to local regulations. The initiative, initially launched in Minnesota, seeks to test consumer demand and operational logistics across diverse regulatory environments.Cann, Wynk, Trail Magic, Stigma, Gigli and Daizy’s will be introduced across Target stores in the three new states, joining the initial Minnesota rollout and expanding the pilot’s geographic footprint.A Target spokesperson said the company is "always exploring new ways to meet our guests’ evolving preferences, grounded in our merchandising authority and focus on thoughtfully curating a relevant assortment." The expansion aims to assess consumer response and logistics as lawmakers consider federal legislation that could reshape a hemp-derived THC market estimated at about $28 billion

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) REFRAMES CREATOR PROGRAMS TO ACCELERATE SOCIAL COMMERCE

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is advancing its social-commerce strategy by introducing two creator initiatives, Club Target and Target Ambassadors, replacing the prior affiliate creator program as of 10th May 2026. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) designed Target Ambassadors for larger influencers and Club Target for smaller creators and everyday shoppers, with the aim of widening the pool of people promoting Target products and boosting visibility and sales across social platforms. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) emphasizes that shopping increasingly happens within the same digital environment, a trend seen in rivals Walmart and Amazon, and seeks to improve discovery and the path to purchase

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) RAISES PRICE TARGET TO $132 AS RBC CAPITAL SIGNALS EARLY TURNAROUND

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Posts early signs of progress in its turnaround as RBC Capital Reports a higher price target of $132 from $130 and an Outperform rating ahead of the Q1 results as of 9th May 2026As part of its strategy, Target rolled out a limited-time collaboration with Parke on April 21, aimed at Gen Z shoppers through Parke’s community-driven approach and focusing on products that reflect consumer preferences to personalize the shopping experienceTarget continues to position itself as a general merchandise retailer offering everyday essentials and differentiated products through both stores and digital channels, with a focus on delivering quality merchandise at competitive prices to its guestsRBC also notes that despite Target’s progress, some AI stocks offer greater upside potential with lower risk, underscoring the varied options investors may considerInvestors will be watching how these efforts translate into performance in the coming quarters

value.Title: IKEA GROUP - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-DGD3-RSP4-R447-00000-00IKEA GROUP - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA GROUP ANNOUNCES NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN, GA

IKEA Group has announced the opening of its new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia. This location is set to open its doors on the 22nd of April, 2026, and will feature a grand opening celebration at 11 a.m. with exclusive tours, giveaways, food sampling, and more.Located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, the new IKEA Newnan is designed to enhance customer accessibility to the brand. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, expressed enthusiasm for the opening, stating, "The Newnan location is just one of the many ways we are expanding our business. We are excited to open our doors and eagerly anticipate welcoming Newnan area residents to this new IKEA experience." The Plan & Order Point provides a more tailored shopping experience. Unlike traditional large-format IKEA stores, this location allows customers to meet with the IKEA team to plan and order home furnishing solutions that require additional assistance, such as kitchens and bathroom layouts. Although customers cannot take products home on the same day, IKEA staff can arrange delivery or facilitate free pick-ups from the convenient onsite location.

+ IKEA GROUP ACCELERATES AI DRIVEN OMNICHANNEL TRANSFORMATION

IKEA Group is accelerating its transition to an omni-channel retail model powered by artificial intelligence. The company says roughly 30 percent of its business now comes from digital channels. On the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, outlined how AI investments are set to reshape both customer experiences and back end operations.The plan features a Bengaluru digital hub that signals a commitment to innovation beyond local operations. India is a dynamic app driven market, and the hub will support local projects as well as global initiatives. The recent acquisition of Locus aims to strengthen last mile delivery and reinforce IKEA's omni-channel approach in the region.AI is also transforming internal processes. AI based design tools have shortened room layout planning from hours to minutes. IKEA Kreativ, an AI driven platform, enables customers to visualize how products fit into their homes and offers several design options at different price levels. Taken together these digital efforts underpin sustainable growth and ethical AI practices as IKEA expands in India and other markets.

+ IKEA FINLAND LAUNCHES DISABILITY-FOCUSED PILOT AT VANTAA STORE

IKEA Group has launched an accessibility pilot at its Vantaa store in Finland, aiming to improve the shopping experience for customers with disabilities. The program began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping services with app-based navigation to bridge digital planning and in-store purchases. Two core features are being tested: a pre-booked 90-minute assisted-shopping session with a trained colleague providing personalized support, and the NaviLens app, which lets customers scan store codes to receive audio guidance on product areas and departmental locations. Both options are free and let shoppers choose their level of help. "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs," says Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland. The company says the pilot is a learning platform to gather feedback and refine the services for possible rollout elsewhere, as part of an ongoing push to create inclusive journeys online and in-store. The effort is supported by IKEA's Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise, and the broader context includes the World Health Organization estimate that about 1.3 billion people globally live with a significant disability.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) LEVERAGES DATA AND INFLUENCERS TO BOOST CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is redefining its marketing approach by merging technology, data analytics and social media influence to drive customer engagement and sales. On 6th May 2026, Molly Battin, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) chief marketing officer, shared insights about the company’s innovative strategies that extend beyond traditional advertising. Battin notes that the shopping journey often begins with urgent home-improvement needs. By using first-party data and regional weather signals, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) tailors product recommendations and messages in real time, ensuring customers receive relevant information at critical moments. The Home Depot app is central to this approach, with app users spending three to four times more than non-users, a trend driven by younger shoppers who favor guided experiences through the app over in-person conversations with sales staff. Features like Store Mode help customers navigate the retailer’s large warehouses more efficiently. The Home Depot is also expanding its creator economy through a creator portal to cultivate partnerships with influencers who connect with DIY enthusiasts and professionals. Battin emphasizes quality over quantity, focusing on creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values. Early campaigns have driven new customer acquisition in niche categories, illustrating how CMOs are becoming more technology-enabled and financially accountable.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) ANALYSTS LIFT PRICE TARGETS ON PRO GROWTH MOMENTUM

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, analysts raised price targets for the company into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS setting a new target at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism tied to the Q4 results and renewed PRO focus. Guggenheim and Wells Fargo cited meaningful progress in Home Depot's PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings on its long-term strategy and ongoing investmentsHowever, some analysts remained cautious on the near-term outlook. RBC Capital and Bernstein held lower targets with neutral views, citing subdued home improvement demand and macro factors that could constrain growth. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets modestly, signaling unease about remodeling demand's impact on revenueIn addition to its revised fiscal 2026 guidance calling for total sales growth of about 2.5% to 4.5%, Home Depot unveiled PRO enhancements, including an expanded Pro digital workspace for project planning and real-time deliveries, and a forthcoming real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value estimate stands at US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions around 4.19% and adjustments to profit margins and future P/E multiples as investors assess the PRO initiatives and guidance

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) POSTS DEBUT IN SAN MARCOS WITH $13 MILLION STORE PROJECT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) Posts Debut in San Marcos With a $13 Million Store Project, Confirmed on 28th April 2026 The project marks the retailer's first location in this rapidly growing college town, which has seen an influx of residents and retail opportunities in recent years. Construction plans have been filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR), and the footprint totals 135,918 square feet including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction slated to begin in September 2026 and finish in August 2027. The site sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and expected to open in October 2026. San Marcos lies on the I-35 corridor beside Texas State University, fueling growth in housing, retail and infrastructure. The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) also reported a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations across the United States, reflecting its ongoing expansion in active markets

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PARTNERS WITH MADE IN TO EXCLUSIVELY LAUNCH CAST IRON COLLECTION

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) has teamed with Made In to launch an exclusive cast iron collection that will be sold only through Williams-Sonoma stores starting 11th May 2026, with the line hitting Made In's direct-to-consumer site by June 1st, in time for Father's Day. Williams-Sonoma will also stock Made In's American-made stainless steel collection, broadening the brand's premium kitchenware lineup. Made In, founded in 2017, serves more than 2,000 restaurants and has seen revenue grow 52% from 2024 to 2025, with a sizable portion of sales in the fourth quarter. The new cast iron line has been refined to be lighter while maintaining performance, addressing consumer interest in non toxic materials and longevity. The partnership followed years of discussions with Williams-Sonoma, aimed at delivering a differentiated product. In addition to Williams-Sonoma, Made In's products are sold at Crate & Barrel, Bloomingdale's and Ace Hardware, and the company plans to establish a European fulfillment center by September. The cast iron launch is a key focus for Made In this year as it works to position itself as a leading provider of premium cookware.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) RECEIVES BUY RATING FROM GOLDMAN SACHS

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a positive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.Based on the projections, Williams-Sonoma is expected to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates. However, non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast to be $13.00, down 33.82% from earlier projections.This upgrade aligns with a broader reassessment of the home goods sector as consumers adjust spending patterns. Williams-Sonoma, a leading player in home furnishings, could benefit from ongoing home-improvement and decor investments as the new financial year approaches.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PROMOTES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO LEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined Williams-Sonoma in 2003 as a district manager for Pottery Barn and has since risen through the Rejuvenation banner, most recently serving as senior vice president since 2020.In her new role, Taylor will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives designed to position Rejuvenation as a stronger pillar within the Williams-Sonoma portfolio. CEO Laura Alber said Taylor’s leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to become a billion-dollar brand, noting her track record, operational excellence, and passion for design.Rejuvenation has expanded its retail footprint with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has pursued collaborations such as a lighting collection with Roll & Hill. Williams-Sonoma has also named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The company continues to expect 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE EXPANDS INTO UPSCALE SEGMENT WITH LUXE COLLECTION

Ashley Furniture Industries is expanding into the upscale segment with the Luxe collection, unveiled May 1, 2026. The move marks a strategic shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores worldwide.Luxe covers living, dining, and bedroom furniture and will be available in about 70% of Ashley's locations, through company-owned outlets, licensees, and online. Sofas run between $1,000 and $1,200, and sectionals range from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, says Luxe broadens Ashley's reach and differentiates the brand in a crowded market. The line relies on Ashley's integrated supply chain, combining domestic manufacturing with imports to keep prices competitive.Dedicated Luxe sections and signage will appear in stores, with the Las Vegas flagship featuring the line prominently. A marketing push featuring Hailee Steinfeld supports the rollout, while CEO Todd Wanek frames Luxe as an evolution that remains within reach.

+ AMAZON EXPANDS NORTHWEST FOOTPRINT WITH $200 MILLION SPANAWAY DISTRIBUTION COMPLEX ACQUISITION

Amazon has acquired Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—a 1.1 million square-foot facility and surrounding parcels—for more than $200 million, a deal reported on the 27th of April 2026.The transaction fits Amazon's ongoing effort to streamline its supply chain and bolster warehousing capacity, a priority as e-commerce grows and customers demand faster deliveries in the Pacific Northwest.Ashley Furniture Industries, a major player in home furnishings, sold the Spanaway property as part of a strategic realignment. The sale may indicate a shift in the company's distribution focus, with implications for its future logistics strategy.

+ FRONTLINEIQ UNVEILS ASHLEY AI ADVANTAGE AT HIGH POINT MARKET, INTRODUCING REAL-TIME SALES COACHING

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the world’s leading AI sales coaching platform purpose-built for in-person sales teams, today announced the Ashley AI Advantage—developed in partnership with Ashley Furniture Industries. The launch occurred at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026, signaling a new era for the home furnishings sector.The Ashley AI Advantage introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time coaching designed for frontline associates, enabling practice with real-world customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven sales methodologies. The program is offered free of charge to Ashley Wholesale customers and Ashley licensees, with options for fully customized deployments for retailers who want to align the solution with their own training playbooks.This rollout positions Ashley as a scalable entry point to FrontlineIQ's platform, pre-configured for the home furnishings industry and available at no cost to Ashley partners. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, said, "This is bigger than coaching—it’s about unlocking the full potential of every frontline sales associate. Partnering with Ashley to bring this to market is a defining moment—not just for our companies, but for retail as a whole."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS PRO FINANCING WITH MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the launch of the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card, developed with Synchrony, to serve professional contractors and extend payment acceptance beyond in-store purchases, following an announcement on the 6th of May 2026. Built to align with Lowe's contractor-focused strategy, the card works anywhere American Express is accepted and aims to deepen relationships with pro buyers across daily business transactions while supporting loyalty and checkout flexibility. Lowe's sets long-term targets in its investor plan, projecting $100.6 billion in revenue and $8.1 billion in earnings by 2029, with expected annual revenue growth of 5.3 percent and earnings rising from $6.6 billion to $8.1 billion. Analysts note the near-term earnings impact may be limited as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides margin discipline amid a flat to low single-digit comparable sales outlook for 2026

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) POSTS FIVE NEW STORE OPENINGS LED BY PORT ST. LUCIE

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts Five New Store Openings Led by Port St. LucieLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space, a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, and will employ more than 100 associates. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms designed to blend style, affordability and innovation. New Lowe's showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Beyond Port St. Lucie, Lowe's has four other openings planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. This expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet the rising demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. Redman noted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is built to deliver value, convenience and the service the community expects from Lowe's. Investors will watch how these locations contribute to overall sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS U.S. FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORE OPENINGS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) expands its U.S. footprint with new store openings, including a Florida location slated for early June 2026 and four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky expected to begin operations later this year. The Port St. Lucie store will span about 94,000 square feet and feature a 30,000-square-foot garden center, employing more than 100 staff members, and will incorporate redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup.Lowe's currently operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, handling roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales surpassed $83 billion. The retailer also emphasizes its MyLowe's Rewards program, which offers points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, supporting loyalty and repeat business, while continuing community initiatives aimed at safe, affordable housing and workforce development

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) BROADENS HEMP-DERIVED THC BEVERAGE DISTRIBUTION AMID REGULATORY AMBIGUITY

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is expanding its hemp-derived THC beverage distribution pilot beyond Minnesota to Florida, Illinois and Texas, with the rollout beginning May 11, 2026, and remaining subject to local regulations. The initiative, initially launched in Minnesota, seeks to test consumer demand and operational logistics across diverse regulatory environments.Cann, Wynk, Trail Magic, Stigma, Gigli and Daizy’s will be introduced across Target stores in the three new states, joining the initial Minnesota rollout and expanding the pilot’s geographic footprint.A Target spokesperson said the company is "always exploring new ways to meet our guests’ evolving preferences, grounded in our merchandising authority and focus on thoughtfully curating a relevant assortment." The expansion aims to assess consumer response and logistics as lawmakers consider federal legislation that could reshape a hemp-derived THC market estimated at about $28 billion

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) REFRAMES CREATOR PROGRAMS TO ACCELERATE SOCIAL COMMERCE

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is advancing its social-commerce strategy by introducing two creator initiatives, Club Target and Target Ambassadors, replacing the prior affiliate creator program as of 10th May 2026. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) designed Target Ambassadors for larger influencers and Club Target for smaller creators and everyday shoppers, with the aim of widening the pool of people promoting Target products and boosting visibility and sales across social platforms. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) emphasizes that shopping increasingly happens within the same digital environment, a trend seen in rivals Walmart and Amazon, and seeks to improve discovery and the path to purchase

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) RAISES PRICE TARGET TO $132 AS RBC CAPITAL SIGNALS EARLY TURNAROUND

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Posts early signs of progress in its turnaround as RBC Capital Reports a higher price target of $132 from $130 and an Outperform rating ahead of the Q1 results as of 9th May 2026As part of its strategy, Target rolled out a limited-time collaboration with Parke on April 21, aimed at Gen Z shoppers through Parke’s community-driven approach and focusing on products that reflect consumer preferences to personalize the shopping experienceTarget continues to position itself as a general merchandise retailer offering everyday essentials and differentiated products through both stores and digital channels, with a focus on delivering quality merchandise at competitive prices to its guestsRBC also notes that despite Target’s progress, some AI stocks offer greater upside potential with lower risk, underscoring the varied options investors may considerInvestors will be watching how these efforts translate into performance in the coming quarters

value.Title: DeKalb Commission defers data center legislation until June

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-8G63-S417-N4YC-00000-00DeKalb Commission defers data center legislation until June2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisDEKALBDeKalb Commission defers data center legislation until JuneZoe Seiler

This story has been updated.

DECATUR, Ga. The DeKalb County Commission, at its May 12 meeting, once again deferred legislation that establishes zoning regulations for data centers. The ordinance is slated for the June 23 agenda.

The county commission plans to hold an in-depth discussion of the pending legislation at a committee of the whole meeting on June 2 at the Lou Walker Senior Center in Lithonia.

The county commission has been discussing data center regulations since September. The goal of the ordinance is to establish land-use regulations. It includes requirements about permitted locations, plans to be submitted with an application, architecture, noise and maintenance.

"This comprehensive land use regulation lays the regulatory foundation for data centers," Deputy Planning Director Rachel Bragg said. "It limits the potential locations for most data centers to industrial zoning districts, requires a SLUP for anything over 100,000 square feet and requires minimum distance requirements from residential zoning districts as well as parks and trails."

It also requires numerous studies and annual reports on noise impact, water consumption and sustainability, energy consumption, lighting, a decommissioning plan, a transmission line impact assessment, tree preservation and reforestation plan, stormwater management plan and sewer plan, Bragg said.

The draft includes a use table that lists four permitted classifications for industrial areas.

There are a few moving parts to the data center discussion. The county commission is considering requiring a baseline assessment of the county's health, socio-economic, and environmental status, and a third-party review of the proposed data center regulations before issuing any land disturbance permit for a data center. Commissioners are also considering allocating $15,000 to Science for Georgia to do this work.

Commissioner Ted Terry introduced a resolution asking the planning department to remove the definition of data center campus, effectively capping any development at 500,000 square feet. That includes an additional request to add incentives for 100 percent renewable energy and to require all applications to obtain a special land-use permit.

All of the data center-related legislation was deferred until June 23.

"You've said no to the text amendment, and we hear you when you say no, so we want to make sure we take the time to not only get what you said in writing, but at the evening COW meeting will be an opportunity for you to speak as well as we go through the text amendment, which is the goal," Deputy Presiding Officer LaDena Bolton said in response to several public comments.

Several residents spoke in opposition to data centers during public comment. As each person spoke, about 40 people stood in support, including a group wearing matching "no data center campus" shirts.

Gina Mangham, Jan Dunaway, Jackie Malcom, Roslind McIntyre and Donna Priest-Brown have formed the DeKalb Citizens Coalition. They continued to advocate for health and environmental impact studies, allowing only small, single-structure data centers, requiring facilities to be at least one mile apart and located away from other public amenities, and mandating regulations for the safe disposal of contaminated water.

They are also asking that the county require special land-use permits for all data center developments and expansions, provide public access to all estimates of water and power usage, and mandate a decommissioning surety bond to ensure a site can be restored if a data center is no longer in use.

"Our version essentially prevents the development of major, large-scale, hyperscale data centers and campuses in unincorporated DeKalb. It's meant to protect all of us, regardless of race or where we live," Mangham said.

No changes have been made to the ordinance since January. A two-year moratorium is the only other acceptable option, Mangham added.

Concerns have been raised about the county's water and sewer systems' ability to handle data center development and associated water usage.

"Allowing data centers under these circumstances is not a risk worth taking for any amount of money. It's downright reckless," Mangham said.

Resident and former county employee Clare Schexnyder added that in the event of a major water interruption, the county has enough water to last about three-to-five hours.

"We can't supply the water that this thing will need. It is an irrational and irresponsible decision," Schexnyder said.

Malcom noted that South DeKalb residents do not want large data centers. They have been advocating against a proposal for a 2-million-square-foot data center in Ellenwood.

"Pollution has no boundaries," Malcom said. "Hyperscale data center campuses will put a strain on our water system and hurt our environment, our health and our quality of life."

Jan Dunaway, a Soil and Water Conservation District Supervisor in DeKalb County, raised concerns about chemically treated water from data centers.

"The concern for sending this hazardous waste into our broken sewer system still exists," Dunaway said. "Our drinking water and sewer infrastructure cannot handle the strain of data centers on our public resources."

South of DeKalb County, neighbors in Fayetteville, Georgia, noticed low water pressure and discovered that a nearby data center had initially used 29 million gallons of water without paying. That is about 44 Olympic-size swimming pools of water, according to Politico. The developer, Quality Technology Services, owed roughly $150,000.

"When the county utility investigated, officials discovered two industrial-scale water hookups feeding a data center campus located 20 miles south of downtown Atlanta," Politico reported. "One water connection had been installed without the utility's knowledge, and the other was not linked to the company's account and therefore wasn't being billed."

In most cases, a data center would require a special land use permit. Priest-Brown said that sounds like accountability, but pushed for better compliance and annual reporting measures.

"If this amendment moves forward without a compliance and audit framework, we are not protecting our communities. We are simply processing permits," Priest-Brown said.

Resident Tina Ross said data centers are not just about buildings, but also their impact on communities.

"My children always tell me that I'm their hero, but lately I don't feel like one, because I look at what's happening across this country, and I feel like we're failing future generations in real time, while calling it development," Ross said. "This is about what kind of world we're handing to our children and our grandchildren."

She added that developments are increasingly near neighborhoods and rural communities across the state and the United States.

"Once these projects arrive, residents begin describing the same pattern over and over again – constant industrial humming, backup generators roaring during testing, massive power substations, bright security lighting throughout the night, heavy truck traffic, stress on local roads, pressure on water infrastructure and rising fears about long-term environmental impact," Ross said.

Renee Cail with Citizens for a Healthy and Safe Environment agreed with other speakers in that residents don't want large data centers in DeKalb County.

"You have heard the community over and over and over and over and over. I don't even know what it's going to take for us to keep these data centers out of our communities," Cail said.

She pointed to Loudoun County, Virginia, which has more than 200 data centers and is home to one of the largest concentrations of such facilities, according to the Loudoun County website.

While the industry has helped diversify Loudoun County's economy, it has also raised concerns about impacts on the region's power grid and noise complaints, Loudoun Now reported.

"What do we have to do to keep the data centers out of our communities, definitely the overburdened and underserved communities. Please listen to your conscience," Cail said. "Keep the data centers out of our communities, please, because we're not going to go anywhere."
