urn:contentItem:6JJG-G2H3-S0TG-H21W-00000-00IKEA GROUP - SECTOR AND PEER NEWS: THE RECENT NEWS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - SECTOR AND PEER NEWS: THE RECENT NEWS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA GROUP ANNOUNCES NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN, GA

IKEA Group has announced the opening of its new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia. This location is set to open its doors on the 22nd of April, 2026, and will feature a grand opening celebration at 11 a.m. with exclusive tours, giveaways, food sampling, and more.Located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, the new IKEA Newnan is designed to enhance customer accessibility to the brand. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, expressed enthusiasm for the opening, stating, "The Newnan location is just one of the many ways we are expanding our business. We are excited to open our doors and eagerly anticipate welcoming Newnan area residents to this new IKEA experience." The Plan & Order Point provides a more tailored shopping experience. Unlike traditional large-format IKEA stores, this location allows customers to meet with the IKEA team to plan and order home furnishing solutions that require additional assistance, such as kitchens and bathroom layouts. Although customers cannot take products home on the same day, IKEA staff can arrange delivery or facilitate free pick-ups from the convenient onsite location.

+ IKEA GROUP ACCELERATES AI DRIVEN OMNICHANNEL TRANSFORMATION

IKEA Group is accelerating its transition to an omni-channel retail model powered by artificial intelligence. The company says roughly 30 percent of its business now comes from digital channels. On the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, outlined how AI investments are set to reshape both customer experiences and back end operations.The plan features a Bengaluru digital hub that signals a commitment to innovation beyond local operations. India is a dynamic app driven market, and the hub will support local projects as well as global initiatives. The recent acquisition of Locus aims to strengthen last mile delivery and reinforce IKEA's omni-channel approach in the region.AI is also transforming internal processes. AI based design tools have shortened room layout planning from hours to minutes. IKEA Kreativ, an AI driven platform, enables customers to visualize how products fit into their homes and offers several design options at different price levels. Taken together these digital efforts underpin sustainable growth and ethical AI practices as IKEA expands in India and other markets.

+ IKEA FINLAND LAUNCHES DISABILITY-FOCUSED PILOT AT VANTAA STORE

IKEA Group has launched an accessibility pilot at its Vantaa store in Finland, aiming to improve the shopping experience for customers with disabilities. The program began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping services with app-based navigation to bridge digital planning and in-store purchases. Two core features are being tested: a pre-booked 90-minute assisted-shopping session with a trained colleague providing personalized support, and the NaviLens app, which lets customers scan store codes to receive audio guidance on product areas and departmental locations. Both options are free and let shoppers choose their level of help. "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs," says Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland. The company says the pilot is a learning platform to gather feedback and refine the services for possible rollout elsewhere, as part of an ongoing push to create inclusive journeys online and in-store. The effort is supported by IKEA's Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise, and the broader context includes the World Health Organization estimate that about 1.3 billion people globally live with a significant disability.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) LEVERAGES DATA AND INFLUENCERS TO BOOST CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is redefining its marketing approach by merging technology, data analytics and social media influence to drive customer engagement and sales. On 6th May 2026, Molly Battin, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) chief marketing officer, shared insights about the company’s innovative strategies that extend beyond traditional advertising. Battin notes that the shopping journey often begins with urgent home-improvement needs. By using first-party data and regional weather signals, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) tailors product recommendations and messages in real time, ensuring customers receive relevant information at critical moments. The Home Depot app is central to this approach, with app users spending three to four times more than non-users, a trend driven by younger shoppers who favor guided experiences through the app over in-person conversations with sales staff. Features like Store Mode help customers navigate the retailer’s large warehouses more efficiently. The Home Depot is also expanding its creator economy through a creator portal to cultivate partnerships with influencers who connect with DIY enthusiasts and professionals. Battin emphasizes quality over quantity, focusing on creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values. Early campaigns have driven new customer acquisition in niche categories, illustrating how CMOs are becoming more technology-enabled and financially accountable.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) ANALYSTS LIFT PRICE TARGETS ON PRO GROWTH MOMENTUM

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, analysts raised price targets for the company into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS setting a new target at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism tied to the Q4 results and renewed PRO focus. Guggenheim and Wells Fargo cited meaningful progress in Home Depot's PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings on its long-term strategy and ongoing investmentsHowever, some analysts remained cautious on the near-term outlook. RBC Capital and Bernstein held lower targets with neutral views, citing subdued home improvement demand and macro factors that could constrain growth. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets modestly, signaling unease about remodeling demand's impact on revenueIn addition to its revised fiscal 2026 guidance calling for total sales growth of about 2.5% to 4.5%, Home Depot unveiled PRO enhancements, including an expanded Pro digital workspace for project planning and real-time deliveries, and a forthcoming real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value estimate stands at US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions around 4.19% and adjustments to profit margins and future P/E multiples as investors assess the PRO initiatives and guidance

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) POSTS DEBUT IN SAN MARCOS WITH $13 MILLION STORE PROJECT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) Posts Debut in San Marcos With a $13 Million Store Project, Confirmed on 28th April 2026 The project marks the retailer's first location in this rapidly growing college town, which has seen an influx of residents and retail opportunities in recent years. Construction plans have been filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR), and the footprint totals 135,918 square feet including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction slated to begin in September 2026 and finish in August 2027. The site sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and expected to open in October 2026. San Marcos lies on the I-35 corridor beside Texas State University, fueling growth in housing, retail and infrastructure. The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) also reported a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations across the United States, reflecting its ongoing expansion in active markets

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PROMOTES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO LEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined Williams-Sonoma in 2003 as a district manager for Pottery Barn and has since risen through the Rejuvenation banner, most recently serving as senior vice president since 2020.In her new role, Taylor will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives designed to position Rejuvenation as a stronger pillar within the Williams-Sonoma portfolio. CEO Laura Alber said Taylor’s leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to become a billion-dollar brand, noting her track record, operational excellence, and passion for design.Rejuvenation has expanded its retail footprint with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has pursued collaborations such as a lighting collection with Roll & Hill. Williams-Sonoma has also named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The company continues to expect 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) REAFFIRMS HOLD RATING AS EVERCORE RAISES TARGET TO $200

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) Reaffirms Hold Rating as Evercore Raises Target to $200 from $195 in a 14th April 2026 update. Evercore maintains its Hold rating on Williams-Sonoma, Inc. while lifting the price target to $200, reflecting a reassessment of growth potential in a competitive home-goods market. Analyst Oliver Wintermantel, who covers Williams-Sonoma at Evercore, has a 66.7% success rate and a 23.9% average return over the past year, according to TipRanks. The hold rating suggests a cautious stance as seasonal trends, inventory management and supply-chain efficiency will influence whether Williams-Sonoma can meet the $200 target in the coming quarters. Evercore's raised target provides investors a reference point in a competitive market.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) RECEIVES BUY RATING FROM GOLDMAN SACHS

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a positive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.Based on the projections, Williams-Sonoma is expected to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates. However, non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast to be $13.00, down 33.82% from earlier projections.This upgrade aligns with a broader reassessment of the home goods sector as consumers adjust spending patterns. Williams-Sonoma, a leading player in home furnishings, could benefit from ongoing home-improvement and decor investments as the new financial year approaches.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE EXPANDS INTO UPSCALE SEGMENT WITH LUXE COLLECTION

Ashley Furniture Industries is expanding into the upscale segment with the Luxe collection, unveiled May 1, 2026. The move marks a strategic shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores worldwide.Luxe covers living, dining, and bedroom furniture and will be available in about 70% of Ashley's locations, through company-owned outlets, licensees, and online. Sofas run between $1,000 and $1,200, and sectionals range from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, says Luxe broadens Ashley's reach and differentiates the brand in a crowded market. The line relies on Ashley's integrated supply chain, combining domestic manufacturing with imports to keep prices competitive.Dedicated Luxe sections and signage will appear in stores, with the Las Vegas flagship featuring the line prominently. A marketing push featuring Hailee Steinfeld supports the rollout, while CEO Todd Wanek frames Luxe as an evolution that remains within reach.

+ FRONTLINEIQ UNVEILS ASHLEY AI ADVANTAGE AT HIGH POINT MARKET, INTRODUCING REAL-TIME SALES COACHING

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the world’s leading AI sales coaching platform purpose-built for in-person sales teams, today announced the Ashley AI Advantage—developed in partnership with Ashley Furniture Industries. The launch occurred at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026, signaling a new era for the home furnishings sector.The Ashley AI Advantage introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time coaching designed for frontline associates, enabling practice with real-world customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven sales methodologies. The program is offered free of charge to Ashley Wholesale customers and Ashley licensees, with options for fully customized deployments for retailers who want to align the solution with their own training playbooks.This rollout positions Ashley as a scalable entry point to FrontlineIQ's platform, pre-configured for the home furnishings industry and available at no cost to Ashley partners. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, said, "This is bigger than coaching—it’s about unlocking the full potential of every frontline sales associate. Partnering with Ashley to bring this to market is a defining moment—not just for our companies, but for retail as a whole."

+ AMAZON EXPANDS NORTHWEST FOOTPRINT WITH $200 MILLION SPANAWAY DISTRIBUTION COMPLEX ACQUISITION

Amazon has acquired Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—a 1.1 million square-foot facility and surrounding parcels—for more than $200 million, a deal reported on the 27th of April 2026.The transaction fits Amazon's ongoing effort to streamline its supply chain and bolster warehousing capacity, a priority as e-commerce grows and customers demand faster deliveries in the Pacific Northwest.Ashley Furniture Industries, a major player in home furnishings, sold the Spanaway property as part of a strategic realignment. The sale may indicate a shift in the company's distribution focus, with implications for its future logistics strategy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS PRO FINANCING WITH MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the launch of the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card, developed with Synchrony, to serve professional contractors and extend payment acceptance beyond in-store purchases, following an announcement on the 6th of May 2026. Built to align with Lowe's contractor-focused strategy, the card works anywhere American Express is accepted and aims to deepen relationships with pro buyers across daily business transactions while supporting loyalty and checkout flexibility. Lowe's sets long-term targets in its investor plan, projecting $100.6 billion in revenue and $8.1 billion in earnings by 2029, with expected annual revenue growth of 5.3 percent and earnings rising from $6.6 billion to $8.1 billion. Analysts note the near-term earnings impact may be limited as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides margin discipline amid a flat to low single-digit comparable sales outlook for 2026

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) POSTS FIVE NEW STORE OPENINGS LED BY PORT ST. LUCIE

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts Five New Store Openings Led by Port St. LucieLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space, a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, and will employ more than 100 associates. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms designed to blend style, affordability and innovation. New Lowe's showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Beyond Port St. Lucie, Lowe's has four other openings planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. This expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet the rising demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. Redman noted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is built to deliver value, convenience and the service the community expects from Lowe's. Investors will watch how these locations contribute to overall sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS U.S. FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORE OPENINGS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) expands its U.S. footprint with new store openings, including a Florida location slated for early June 2026 and four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky expected to begin operations later this year. The Port St. Lucie store will span about 94,000 square feet and feature a 30,000-square-foot garden center, employing more than 100 staff members, and will incorporate redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup.Lowe's currently operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, handling roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales surpassed $83 billion. The retailer also emphasizes its MyLowe's Rewards program, which offers points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, supporting loyalty and repeat business, while continuing community initiatives aimed at safe, affordable housing and workforce development

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXPANDS NEXT-DAY DELIVERY TO 60% OF U.S. POPULATION, REACHES 50 MAJOR METRO AREAS

As Of The 28th Of April 2026, Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Expands Next-Day Delivery To 60% Of The U.S. Population, Reaching More Than 50 Major Metropolitan Areas, With Many Deliveries Offered FreeThe Rollout Increases The Eligible Item Pool To 85% Of All Products Sold In Stores, And Allows Customers To Place Orders As Late As 6 P.M. Local Time For Next-Day Arrival, While Standard Delivery Is $5.99 And Free Delivery Applies To Orders Over $35 In Eligible MarketsThis Initiative Is Part Of The New Chapter $5 Billion Strategy Led By Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke, Aimed At Refreshing Stores, Launching New Collaborations, And Strengthening Local Communities Following A 1.7% Net Sales Decline In 2025

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) E-COMMERCE SHARE REACHES 20.6% OF MERCHANDISE SALES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) posts fiscal 2025 results for the period ending 31st January 2026, with the stock up 29% since the start of 2026.\n\nE-commerce sales reach 20.6% of total merchandise sales, a record share, up from nearly 18% during the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020. In the fourth quarter, e-commerce represented 23.7% of merchandise sales.\n\nNon-merchandise sales rose 25% in the same quarter, driven by membership revenue which more than doubled year over year. Target operates as a general merchandise retailer with physical stores and digital platforms, blending everyday essentials with discounted differentiated merchandise to attract customers. Analysts note that opportunities in AI and other sectors may offer upside

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXPANDS BULLSEYE BUILDS WITH COMMUNITY PROGRAM ACROSS 13 CITIES BACKED BY $1 MILLION INVESTMENT

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) leads the Bullseye Builds with Community Program, a design-driven effort to transform community spaces across 13 U.S. cities. Backed by a $1 million investment, the rollout will officially kick off on 21st May 2026 in Denver and Las VegasThe program, launched in 2024, has completed 25 projects to date and has positively impacted over 250,000 community members with the help of Target team volunteers. In Denver, Target will partner with Trevista at Horace Mann Elementary School to upgrade the student wellness room and create outdoor learning spaces, featuring murals and new furniture. In Las Vegas, Target will team with The Just One Project to address food insecurity and enhance shopping areas and pantry facilities. Each project will involve more than 100 volunteers from TargetAdditional cities slated for this year’s Bullseye Builds include Austin, Birmingham, Columbus, Jersey City, Logan Township, Orlando, Washington, D.C.; Minneapolis-St. Paul; Phoenix; St. Louis; and Seattle. Kiera Fernandez, Executive Vice President and Chief Community and Stakeholder Engagement Officer, emphasized the importance of local insights in shaping these projects: "At Target, we believe investing in the places we call home builds strong communities and strong partnerships." The company also noted that team members contributed 1 million volunteer hours in the U.S. in 2025

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHD-CNJ3-S80X-K431-00000-00Who was Ted Turner? Media mogul dead at 872026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisENTERTAINMENT: LATEST, ENTERTAINMENT: LATEST & NEWSPAPER PUBLISHING NEWSWho was Ted Turner? Media mogul dead at 87Jenna Prestininzi and Bryan Alexander, USA TODAY NETWORK

Media mogul Ted Turner, known for founding CNN and Turner Classic Movies, died Wednesday, May 6, at age 87.

Turner died with his family around him, according to a Turner Enterprises press release. A prominent media businessman and philanthropist, his empire expanded to include CNN, TCM, Cartoon Network, TNT, TBS, Ted's Montana Grill, the Turner Foundation and other ventures.

"Ted was an intensely involved and committed leader, intrepid, fearless and always willing to back a hunch and trust his own judgement," Mark Thompson, chairman and CEO of CNN Worldwide, said in a statement. "He was and always will be the presiding spirit of CNN. Ted is the giant on whose shoulders we stand, and we will all take a moment today to recognize him and his impact on our lives and the world."

Turner is survived by his five children, fourteen grandchildren and two great-grandchildren.

"A private family service is planned, and a public memorial will be held at a later date, with details to follow," Turner Enterprises said in its statement.

Here's what to know about Ted Turner.

When did Ted Turner die?

Ted Turner died Wednesday, May 6, at 87, "surrounded by his family," according to a Turner Enterprises news release obtained by USA TODAY.

Who was Ted Turner?

Born Robert Edward Turner III in Cincinnati and raised in Georgia, known as Ted Turner, he was a businessman, media mogul and philanthropist known for founding CNN as the first global 24-hour cable news station in 1980, according to Turner Enterprises.

Turner also founded the television stations TBS, TCM, TNT and Cartoon Network and launched two independent film production companies.

Turner was married to actress and activist Jane Fonda for 10 years, from 1991 until the couple divorced in 2001.

He started Ted's Montana Grill, an American restaurant chain specializing in Montana bar-style, serving beef or bison burgers alongside cold brews. His Ted Turner Reserves offer a tranquil stay surrounded by New Mexico's great outdoors.

Delving into sports, Turner was a longtime owner of the Atlanta Braves and the Atlanta Hawks.

His philanthropical legacy included chairing the Turner Foundation, which donated to environmental causes and promoted sustainable energy, and establishing the Turner Renewable Energy company, Turner Enterprises noted. Turner also founded the United Nations Foundation and the Nuclear Threat Initiative, advocating for global peace and prosperity.

USA TODAY contributed.

Contact Jenna Prestininzi: jprestininzi@freepress.com.

This article originally appeared on Detroit Free Press: Who was Ted Turner? Media mogul dead at 87

urn:contentItem:6JK4-WBY3-S09D-H52F-00000-00IKEA GROUP - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA EXPANDS U.S. FOOTPRINT WITH NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN

IKEA Group has announced the opening of its Newnan Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia. The location is set to open on the 22nd of April, 2026, and will feature a grand opening at 11 a.m. with exclusive tours, giveaways, food sampling, and more.Located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, the new IKEA Newnan is designed to enhance customer accessibility to the brand. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, said the Newnan location is one of the many ways the company is expanding, adding, \"We are excited to open our doors and eagerly anticipate welcoming Newnan area residents to this new IKEA experience.\" The Newnan Plan & Order Point offers planning assistance for home projects that require more help, such as kitchens and bathroom layouts. Customers meet with the IKEA team to plan and order home furnishings, but same-day pickup is not available; delivery or onsite free pickup can be arranged.In addition to the new format, IKEA Group is investing in e-commerce capabilities, including new IKEA Family benefits, improved shipping and delivery options, Click & Collect services, and TaskRabbit assembly assistance to support both in-store and online shopping.The expansion underscores IKEA's strategy to meet evolving consumer needs by adding accessible planning formats and convenient services as it grows its U.S. footprint.

+ INGKA GROUP BETS ON AI TO REDEFINE RETAIL IN INDIA

IKEA Group is pressing ahead with an AI enabled transformation of its omni-channel retail model, with digital channels now accounting for about 30 percent of the business. On the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, described how AI investments are redefining the customer journey and boosting operational efficiency across markets.In India, the company is building a Bengaluru digital hub to fuel innovation beyond local operations and support global initiatives. The acquisition of Locus is intended to strengthen last mile delivery capabilities, reinforcing IKEA's omni-channel ambitions in one of the world's most dynamic app driven markets.AI tools are also changing internal workflows. AI based design tools dramatically reduce the time coworkers spend planning rooms for customers, from hours to minutes. IKEA Kreativ lets customers visualize products in their homes, offering multiple design options at varying price levels.IKEA's ongoing digital investments are tied to a broader push for sustainable growth. The company plans to expand its digital footprint in India, leveraging a thriving startup ecosystem, while emphasizing ethical AI practices to maintain customer trust.

+ IKEA TESTS ASSISTED SHOPPING AND NAVILENS NAVIGATION TO AID CUSTOMERS WITH DISABILITIES IN FINLAND

Situated at the Vantaa store in Finland, IKEA Group's new accessibility pilot began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping with NaviLens navigation to help customers with disabilities bridge online planning and in-store purchases. The program centers on two offerings: a 90-minute assisted-shopping session that customers can book in advance, with a trained coworker providing tailored support throughout the shopping trip, and the NaviLens app that guides shoppers by scanning codes around the store to provide audio cues about product areas and department locations. Both services are free, reflecting IKEA's intent to remove barriers and give customers control over the level of assistance they receive. "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs," says Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland. IKEA frames the pilot as a learning platform to gather feedback and refine the services for potential deployment in other locations. The initiative sits within the company's broader push to create inclusive customer journeys, both online and in-store, supported by its Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise. The World Health Organization notes that about 1.3 billion people globally live with significant disability, underscoring the importance of accessibility in retail.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) INTEGRATES FIRST-PARTY DATA AND CREATOR NETWORKS TO PERSONALIZE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE

On 6th May 2026, Molly Battin, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) chief marketing officer, outlined how the retailer is integrating technology, data analytics and influencer partnerships to engage customers and drive sales. The company relies on first-party data and regional weather signals to tailor its product recommendations and messages in real time, ensuring relevance at critical moments. The Home Depot app remains central to the strategy, with customers who use the app reportedly spending three to four times more than non-users. This pattern is especially pronounced among younger shoppers who prefer app-guided experiences over direct conversations with sales staff. Features such as Store Mode help customers navigate the stores more efficiently. Beyond technology, The Home Depot is tapping the creator economy through a creator portal to cultivate partnerships with influencers who resonate with DIY enthusiasts and professionals. Battin says the emphasis is on quality over quantity, choosing creators who authentically connect with the brand and can convey its values. The campaigns tied to this approach have driven new customer acquisition in niche categories, underscoring a shift in the role of CMOs toward more technical and financially accountable marketing, with digital tools, targeted messages and influencer partnerships shaping how the company engages customers.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) PRO INITIATIVES DRIVE INVESTOR OPTIMISM DESPITE MIXED NEAR-TERM OUTLOOK

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, analysts updated price targets for the stock, sending targets into the high US$300s to mid US$400s as confidence grows around the companys renewed PRO strategy. UBS paced the revisions with a US$450 target, while Jefferies set US$454, underscoring a shift in sentiment following a better-than-expected Q4 and ongoing PRO investmentsNonetheless, a portion of the research community remains cautious about the near term. RBC Capital and Bernstein keep neutral ratings and lower targets, pointing to softer home improvement demand and macro headwinds. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed their targets slightly, reflecting concerns about remodeling revenue trajectoriesBeyond its 2026 guidance for total sales growth of about 2.5% to 4.5%, Home Depot is expanding tools for professionals, including a broader Pro digital workspace and a real-time delivery tracker for large materials, aimed at improving service levels. The stock’s fair value is pegged at US$408.21, with long-run revenue growth around 4.19% and some adjustments to margins and future P/E multiples as the PRO strategy unfolds

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) SETS PLANS FOR $13 MILLION SAN MARCOS STORE, EXPANDING TEXAS FOOTPRINT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) Posts its debut in San Marcos with a $13 million store project confirmed on 28th April 2026. This marks the retailer's first location in the growing college town, which has attracted residents and retail growth in recent years. Plans filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) show a footprint of 135,918 square feet, comprising a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction slated to begin in September 2026 and to be completed in August 2027. The site is located near a third H-E-B grocery store under construction, expected to open in October 2026. San Marcos, positioned on the I-35 corridor and home to Texas State University, has seen substantial growth in housing, retail and infrastructure. The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) reported a 3.2% rise in fiscal year 2025 and operates about 2,359 locations across the United States

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) SIGNS EXCLUSIVE CAST IRON COLLABORATION WITH MADE IN AND EXPANDS STAINLESS STEEL LINE

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) deepens its premium cookware lineup by signing an exclusive cast iron collection with Made In, set to debut in Williams-Sonoma stores on 11th May 2026 and become available on Made In's direct-to-consumer site by June 1st. In the same collaboration, Williams-Sonoma will stock Made In's American-made stainless steel collection, expanding both brands' offerings. Made In, founded in 2017, counts more than 2,000 restaurants as customers and posted revenue growth of 52% from 2024 to 2025, with a sizable share of sales in the fourth quarter. The new cast iron line has been refined to be lighter without sacrificing performance, addressing consumer demand for durable, non toxic options. Discussions with Williams-Sonoma spanned several years, and the pair sought to deliver a differentiated product before moving forward. In addition to Williams-Sonoma, Made In's products are sold at Crate & Barrel, Bloomingdale's and Ace Hardware, and the company plans to establish a European fulfillment center by September as part of its international rollout. The launch underlines Made In's strategy to broaden its retail footprint while establishing itself as a leading name in premium cookware.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) GOLDMAN SACHS UPGRADES TO BUY ON OPTIMISTIC OUTLOOK

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a positive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.The investment bank projects Williams-Sonoma to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, representing a 6.20% increase from prior estimates. Non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast to be $13.00, down 33.82% from earlier projections.The upgrade comes amid a broader reassessment of the home goods sector as consumer spending patterns shift. Williams-Sonoma, a key player in home furnishings, may benefit from continued investments in home improvement and decor, even as margins face pressure.Investors will be closely watching how the company executes its growth plan and whether the earnings trajectory aligns with Goldman Sachs’ constructive view.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) ELEVATES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO PRESIDENT OF REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined the company in 2003 as a district manager for Pottery Barn and advanced through the Rejuvenation banner, most recently serving as senior vice president since 2020.In the new post, Taylor will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives aimed at strengthening Rejuvenation's role within the Williams-Sonoma portfolio.CEO Laura Alber highlighted Taylor's leadership as instrumental in positioning Rejuvenation for potential billion-dollar growth, citing her track record, operational discipline, and design focus.Rejuvenation has expanded its footprint with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has pursued collaborations, including a lighting collection with Roll & Hill.Williams-Sonoma has also renewed its leadership team with Vicki McWilliams named president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch named chief marketing officer.The company maintains an outlook for 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ SETTLEMENT REACHED IN MATTRESS FIBERGLASS CASE INVOLVING ASHLEY FURNITURE AND RESIDENT HOME, WORTH $9 MILLION

Ashley Furniture Industries LLC and Resident Home LLC have agreed to a $9 million class action settlement related to certain mattresses sold from October 1, 2017 through June 30, 2024. The claims center on fiberglass used as a fire retardant, which could escape and create harm, with eligible buyers set to receive vouchers. Eligible class members are those who purchased affected mattresses designed, manufactured, or sold by Ashley Furniture and Resident Home during the period. The settlement addresses claims of economic loss, property damage, and physical harm arising from the products. Claim deadline is July 17, 2026. Vouchers will be issued on a pro rata basis, calculated by dividing the total settlement fund by the number of valid claims. Vouchers can be used on purchases from websites such as www.AshleyFurniture.com and www.NectarSleep.com, and any funds remaining after distribution may be paid as cash. For those filing a claim, documentation is required. Claimants who received an email with a unique ID and PIN will need to complete only part of the claim form. Others must provide proof of purchase such as receipts, store records, or photos of mattress labels, or other purchase details if direct proof is unavailable. Ashley Furniture and Resident Home have agreed to settle to resolve the lawsuit without admitting wrongdoing. The final approval hearing is scheduled for September 24, 2026, and vouchers will be distributed after any appeals are resolved.

+ ASHLEY LUXE LAUNCH TARGETS NEW DEMOGRAPHIC WHILE MAINTAINING CORE FOCUS

Ashley Furniture Industries is expanding into the upscale segment with the Luxe collection, unveiled May 1, 2026. The rollout marks a deliberate pivot for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores and has built a reputation on accessible, midrange pieces while maintaining a strong domestic footprint. The move places Luxe within a broader effort to refresh the brand and broaden its appeal to a more diverse audience.Luxe is a full-line assortment including living, dining, and bedroom furniture and will be available in roughly 70% of Ashley's locations, through company-owned stores, licensees, and online. The pricing places Luxe above entry-level inventories but below luxury labels such as Arhaus and RH, signaling a measured shift toward higher price points without a wholesale change in positioning.Sofas are priced between $1,000 and $1,200, and many sectionals range from $2,900 to $6,000, a spectrum designed to attract aspirational buyers who want style without paying premium-brand prices.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience at Ashley Global Retail, says Luxe lets the company extend its reach and differentiate itself in a crowded market. Ashley Luxe is a way for us to extend our reach to new customers and for us to differentiate ourselves, she noted.The collection leverages Ashley's existing supply chain by blending domestic manufacturing with imports to maintain competitive pricing. Stores will add dedicated Luxe sections with signage and merchandising, and the Las Vegas flagship will spotlight the line as a focal point of the showroom.

+ AMAZON BUYS SPANAWAY FACILITY FROM ASHLEY FURNITURE TO BOLSTER PACIFIC NORTHWEST LOGISTICS

Amazon has acquired Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex, a 1.1 million square-foot facility and adjacent parcels, for more than $200 million, according to a report filed on the 27th of April 2026.The purchase underscores Amazon's strategy to tighten its supply chain and expand warehousing capacity as e-commerce growth continues, with the Spanaway site positioned to support delivery operations in Washington and beyond.Ashley Furniture Industries sells the property as part of a strategic realignment, signaling a possible shift in its distribution strategy.The deal reflects the competitive demand for industrial space in the Pacific Northwest and suggests Amazon's continued confidence in the logistics sector amid rising online shopping.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) DEBUTS PRO AMEX CARD TO DEEPEN CONTRACTOR RELATIONSHIPS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card introduction, created with Synchrony, for professional contractors and usable anywhere American Express is accepted, following the announcement on the 6th of May 2026. The expanded payment option broadens Lowe's reach beyond in-store purchases and is designed to capture a larger share of the pro contractor market. Built to align with Lowe's contractor-focused strategy, the card expands payment options beyond stores and aims to deepen relationships with pro buyers across daily business transactions while supporting loyalty and checkout flexibility. The company maintains 2026 guidance for flat to low single-digit comparable sales and targets revenue of $100.6 billion and earnings of $8.1 billion by 2029, supported by 5.3% annual revenue growth. Analysts caution that the near-term earnings impact may be modest and highlight elevated debt from the FBM acquisition as a potential constraint. Lowe's notes it will need to integrate recent acquisitions while managing margins in a competitive contractor market

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) SETS PORT ST. LUCIE STORE LAUNCH AS FIRST OF FIVE NEW OPENINGS IN 2026

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Sets Port St. Lucie Store Launch As First Of Five New Openings In 2026Lowe's is set to open the first of five stores planned for 2026, with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location scheduled for April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will encompass approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space, alongside a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, and will employ more than 100 associates. This location will be Lowe's second in Port St. Lucie and will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms aimed at blending style, affordability and innovation. The upgraded showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New stores will also include self-service paint kiosks and buy online, pick up in-store zones.In addition to Port St. Lucie, four more openings are planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. The expansion aligns with Lowe's broader effort to meet rising demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. Redman said Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is built to deliver value, convenience and the service the community expects from Lowe's. Investors will monitor how these locations contribute to overall sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) PLANS FLORIDA OPENINGS AND TEXAS/KENTUCKY EXPANSIONS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) discloses a multi-state expansion, including a Florida location to open in early June 2026 and four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky expected to begin operations later this year. The Port St. Lucie store will cover about 94,000 square feet with a 30,000-square-foot garden center and will hire more than 100 staff.The new locations will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for quick paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup, enhancing convenience for customers.Lowe's currently operates more than 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, and handles roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, Lowe's reported sales above $83 billion. The MyLowe's Rewards program offers points on eligible purchases and membership tiers based on spending, supporting customer loyalty.Founded in the early 1920s and headquartered in Mooresville, North Carolina, Lowe's continues community initiatives focused on safe, affordable housing and workforce development as it expands its footprint

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXPANDS HEMP-DERIVED THC BEVERAGE PILOT ACROSS FLORIDA, ILLINOIS AND TEXAS

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is expanding its hemp-derived THC beverage distribution pilot beyond Minnesota to Florida, Illinois and Texas, with the rollout beginning May 11, 2026, and remaining subject to local regulations. The initiative, which originally launched in Minnesota, now extends to three additional states as part of a broader testing phase.The expansion broadens the pilot’s geographic footprint, enabling Target to evaluate performance across varying regulatory environments and store formats.Cann, Wynk, Trail Magic, Stigma, Gigli and Daizy’s will be introduced across Target stores in the three new states, alongside the Minnesota locations already in the program.A Target spokesperson said the company is "always exploring new ways to meet our guests’ evolving preferences, grounded in our merchandising authority and focus on thoughtfully curating a relevant assortment." The move is designed to gauge consumer demand and logistics while federal legislation could significantly alter the market for hemp-derived products, currently estimated at about $28 billion

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) UNVEILS CLUB TARGET AND AMBASSADORS TO EXPAND CREATOR NETWORK

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is advancing its social-commerce strategy by introducing two creator initiatives, Club Target and Target Ambassadors, replacing the prior affiliate creator program as of 10th May 2026. Club Target targets smaller creators and everyday shoppers, while Target Ambassadors targets larger influencers, a dual approach designed to broaden the pool of Target advocates and enhance engagement across platforms. The company expects these initiatives to improve product discovery and shorten the path to purchase within social feeds

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) REPORTS EARLY PROGRESS AS RBC CAPITAL SETS $132 TARGET

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Posts early signs of progress in its turnaround as RBC Capital Reports a higher price target of $132 from $130 and an Outperform rating ahead of the Q1 results on 9th May 2026The note signals cautious optimism that the retailer's initiatives are resonating with guestsOn April 21, Target rolled out a limited-time collaboration with Parke, a premium brand appealing to Gen Z shoppers, through its community-driven approach, focusing on products that reflect consumer preferences to personalize the shopping experienceTarget continues to position itself as a general merchandise retailer offering everyday essentials and differentiated products through both stores and digital channels, with a goal to deliver quality merchandise at competitive prices to its guestsRBC notes that while Target has momentum, some AI stocks offer greater upside potential with lower risk, underscoring a broader investment landscape for investorsInvestors will monitor how these efforts translate into near-term results in the coming quarters

urn:contentItem:6JJP-F6J3-RS96-T52M-00000-00IKEA GROUP - CURRENT DEVELOPMENTS IN THE SECTOR AND PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - CURRENT DEVELOPMENTS IN THE SECTOR AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA GROUP ANNOUNCES NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN, GA

IKEA Group has announced the opening of its new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia. This location is set to open its doors on the 22nd of April, 2026, and will feature a grand opening celebration at 11 a.m. with exclusive tours, giveaways, food sampling, and more.Located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, the new IKEA Newnan is designed to enhance customer accessibility to the brand. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, expressed enthusiasm for the opening, stating, "The Newnan location is just one of the many ways we are expanding our business. We are excited to open our doors and eagerly anticipate welcoming Newnan area residents to this new IKEA experience." The Plan & Order Point provides a more tailored shopping experience. Unlike traditional large-format IKEA stores, this location allows customers to meet with the IKEA team to plan and order home furnishing solutions that require additional assistance, such as kitchens and bathroom layouts. Although customers cannot take products home on the same day, IKEA staff can arrange delivery or facilitate free pick-ups from the convenient onsite location.

+ IKEA GROUP ACCELERATES AI DRIVEN OMNICHANNEL TRANSFORMATION

IKEA Group is accelerating its transition to an omni-channel retail model powered by artificial intelligence. The company says roughly 30 percent of its business now comes from digital channels. On the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, outlined how AI investments are set to reshape both customer experiences and back end operations.The plan features a Bengaluru digital hub that signals a commitment to innovation beyond local operations. India is a dynamic app driven market, and the hub will support local projects as well as global initiatives. The recent acquisition of Locus aims to strengthen last mile delivery and reinforce IKEA's omni-channel approach in the region.AI is also transforming internal processes. AI based design tools have shortened room layout planning from hours to minutes. IKEA Kreativ, an AI driven platform, enables customers to visualize how products fit into their homes and offers several design options at different price levels. Taken together these digital efforts underpin sustainable growth and ethical AI practices as IKEA expands in India and other markets.

+ IKEA FINLAND LAUNCHES DISABILITY-FOCUSED PILOT AT VANTAA STORE

IKEA Group has launched an accessibility pilot at its Vantaa store in Finland, aiming to improve the shopping experience for customers with disabilities. The program began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping services with app-based navigation to bridge digital planning and in-store purchases. Two core features are being tested: a pre-booked 90-minute assisted-shopping session with a trained colleague providing personalized support, and the NaviLens app, which lets customers scan store codes to receive audio guidance on product areas and departmental locations. Both options are free and let shoppers choose their level of help. "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs," says Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland. The company says the pilot is a learning platform to gather feedback and refine the services for possible rollout elsewhere, as part of an ongoing push to create inclusive journeys online and in-store. The effort is supported by IKEA's Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise, and the broader context includes the World Health Organization estimate that about 1.3 billion people globally live with a significant disability.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) LEVERAGES DATA AND INFLUENCERS TO BOOST CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is redefining its marketing approach by merging technology, data analytics and social media influence to drive customer engagement and sales. On 6th May 2026, Molly Battin, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) chief marketing officer, shared insights about the company’s innovative strategies that extend beyond traditional advertising. Battin notes that the shopping journey often begins with urgent home-improvement needs. By using first-party data and regional weather signals, The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) tailors product recommendations and messages in real time, ensuring customers receive relevant information at critical moments. The Home Depot app is central to this approach, with app users spending three to four times more than non-users, a trend driven by younger shoppers who favor guided experiences through the app over in-person conversations with sales staff. Features like Store Mode help customers navigate the retailer’s large warehouses more efficiently. The Home Depot is also expanding its creator economy through a creator portal to cultivate partnerships with influencers who connect with DIY enthusiasts and professionals. Battin emphasizes quality over quantity, focusing on creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values. Early campaigns have driven new customer acquisition in niche categories, illustrating how CMOs are becoming more technology-enabled and financially accountable.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) ANALYSTS LIFT PRICE TARGETS ON PRO GROWTH MOMENTUM

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, analysts raised price targets for the company into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS setting a new target at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism tied to the Q4 results and renewed PRO focus. Guggenheim and Wells Fargo cited meaningful progress in Home Depot's PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings on its long-term strategy and ongoing investmentsHowever, some analysts remained cautious on the near-term outlook. RBC Capital and Bernstein held lower targets with neutral views, citing subdued home improvement demand and macro factors that could constrain growth. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets modestly, signaling unease about remodeling demand's impact on revenueIn addition to its revised fiscal 2026 guidance calling for total sales growth of about 2.5% to 4.5%, Home Depot unveiled PRO enhancements, including an expanded Pro digital workspace for project planning and real-time deliveries, and a forthcoming real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value estimate stands at US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions around 4.19% and adjustments to profit margins and future P/E multiples as investors assess the PRO initiatives and guidance

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) POSTS DEBUT IN SAN MARCOS WITH $13 MILLION STORE PROJECT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) Posts Debut in San Marcos With a $13 Million Store Project, Confirmed on 28th April 2026 The project marks the retailer's first location in this rapidly growing college town, which has seen an influx of residents and retail opportunities in recent years. Construction plans have been filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR), and the footprint totals 135,918 square feet including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction slated to begin in September 2026 and finish in August 2027. The site sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and expected to open in October 2026. San Marcos lies on the I-35 corridor beside Texas State University, fueling growth in housing, retail and infrastructure. The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) also reported a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations across the United States, reflecting its ongoing expansion in active markets

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PROMOTES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO LEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined Williams-Sonoma in 2003 as a district manager for Pottery Barn and has since risen through the Rejuvenation banner, most recently serving as senior vice president since 2020.In her new role, Taylor will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives designed to position Rejuvenation as a stronger pillar within the Williams-Sonoma portfolio. CEO Laura Alber said Taylor’s leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to become a billion-dollar brand, noting her track record, operational excellence, and passion for design.Rejuvenation has expanded its retail footprint with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has pursued collaborations such as a lighting collection with Roll & Hill. Williams-Sonoma has also named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The company continues to expect 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) REAFFIRMS HOLD RATING AS EVERCORE RAISES TARGET TO $200

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) Reaffirms Hold Rating as Evercore Raises Target to $200 from $195 in a 14th April 2026 update. Evercore maintains its Hold rating on Williams-Sonoma, Inc. while lifting the price target to $200, reflecting a reassessment of growth potential in a competitive home-goods market. Analyst Oliver Wintermantel, who covers Williams-Sonoma at Evercore, has a 66.7% success rate and a 23.9% average return over the past year, according to TipRanks. The hold rating suggests a cautious stance as seasonal trends, inventory management and supply-chain efficiency will influence whether Williams-Sonoma can meet the $200 target in the coming quarters. Evercore's raised target provides investors a reference point in a competitive market.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) RECEIVES BUY RATING FROM GOLDMAN SACHS

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a positive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.Based on the projections, Williams-Sonoma is expected to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates. However, non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast to be $13.00, down 33.82% from earlier projections.This upgrade aligns with a broader reassessment of the home goods sector as consumers adjust spending patterns. Williams-Sonoma, a leading player in home furnishings, could benefit from ongoing home-improvement and decor investments as the new financial year approaches.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE EXPANDS INTO UPSCALE SEGMENT WITH LUXE COLLECTION

Ashley Furniture Industries is expanding into the upscale segment with the Luxe collection, unveiled May 1, 2026. The move marks a strategic shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores worldwide.Luxe covers living, dining, and bedroom furniture and will be available in about 70% of Ashley's locations, through company-owned outlets, licensees, and online. Sofas run between $1,000 and $1,200, and sectionals range from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, says Luxe broadens Ashley's reach and differentiates the brand in a crowded market. The line relies on Ashley's integrated supply chain, combining domestic manufacturing with imports to keep prices competitive.Dedicated Luxe sections and signage will appear in stores, with the Las Vegas flagship featuring the line prominently. A marketing push featuring Hailee Steinfeld supports the rollout, while CEO Todd Wanek frames Luxe as an evolution that remains within reach.

+ AMAZON EXPANDS NORTHWEST FOOTPRINT WITH $200 MILLION SPANAWAY DISTRIBUTION COMPLEX ACQUISITION

Amazon has acquired Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—a 1.1 million square-foot facility and surrounding parcels—for more than $200 million, a deal reported on the 27th of April 2026.The transaction fits Amazon's ongoing effort to streamline its supply chain and bolster warehousing capacity, a priority as e-commerce grows and customers demand faster deliveries in the Pacific Northwest.Ashley Furniture Industries, a major player in home furnishings, sold the Spanaway property as part of a strategic realignment. The sale may indicate a shift in the company's distribution focus, with implications for its future logistics strategy.

+ FRONTLINEIQ UNVEILS ASHLEY AI ADVANTAGE AT HIGH POINT MARKET, INTRODUCING REAL-TIME SALES COACHING

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the world’s leading AI sales coaching platform purpose-built for in-person sales teams, today announced the Ashley AI Advantage—developed in partnership with Ashley Furniture Industries. The launch occurred at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026, signaling a new era for the home furnishings sector.The Ashley AI Advantage introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time coaching designed for frontline associates, enabling practice with real-world customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven sales methodologies. The program is offered free of charge to Ashley Wholesale customers and Ashley licensees, with options for fully customized deployments for retailers who want to align the solution with their own training playbooks.This rollout positions Ashley as a scalable entry point to FrontlineIQ's platform, pre-configured for the home furnishings industry and available at no cost to Ashley partners. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, said, "This is bigger than coaching—it’s about unlocking the full potential of every frontline sales associate. Partnering with Ashley to bring this to market is a defining moment—not just for our companies, but for retail as a whole."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS PRO FINANCING WITH MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the launch of the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card, developed with Synchrony, to serve professional contractors and extend payment acceptance beyond in-store purchases, following an announcement on the 6th of May 2026. Built to align with Lowe's contractor-focused strategy, the card works anywhere American Express is accepted and aims to deepen relationships with pro buyers across daily business transactions while supporting loyalty and checkout flexibility. Lowe's sets long-term targets in its investor plan, projecting $100.6 billion in revenue and $8.1 billion in earnings by 2029, with expected annual revenue growth of 5.3 percent and earnings rising from $6.6 billion to $8.1 billion. Analysts note the near-term earnings impact may be limited as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides margin discipline amid a flat to low single-digit comparable sales outlook for 2026

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) POSTS FIVE NEW STORE OPENINGS LED BY PORT ST. LUCIE

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts Five New Store Openings Led by Port St. LucieLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space, a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, and will employ more than 100 associates. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms designed to blend style, affordability and innovation. New Lowe's showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Beyond Port St. Lucie, Lowe's has four other openings planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. This expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet the rising demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. Redman noted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is built to deliver value, convenience and the service the community expects from Lowe's. Investors will watch how these locations contribute to overall sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS U.S. FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORE OPENINGS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) expands its U.S. footprint with new store openings, including a Florida location slated for early June 2026 and four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky expected to begin operations later this year. The Port St. Lucie store will span about 94,000 square feet and feature a 30,000-square-foot garden center, employing more than 100 staff members, and will incorporate redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup.Lowe's currently operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, handling roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales surpassed $83 billion. The retailer also emphasizes its MyLowe's Rewards program, which offers points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, supporting loyalty and repeat business, while continuing community initiatives aimed at safe, affordable housing and workforce development

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXPANDS NEXT-DAY DELIVERY TO 60% OF U.S. POPULATION, REACHES 50 MAJOR METRO AREAS

As Of The 28th Of April 2026, Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Expands Next-Day Delivery To 60% Of The U.S. Population, Reaching More Than 50 Major Metropolitan Areas, With Many Deliveries Offered FreeThe Rollout Increases The Eligible Item Pool To 85% Of All Products Sold In Stores, And Allows Customers To Place Orders As Late As 6 P.M. Local Time For Next-Day Arrival, While Standard Delivery Is $5.99 And Free Delivery Applies To Orders Over $35 In Eligible MarketsThis Initiative Is Part Of The New Chapter $5 Billion Strategy Led By Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke, Aimed At Refreshing Stores, Launching New Collaborations, And Strengthening Local Communities Following A 1.7% Net Sales Decline In 2025

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) E-COMMERCE SHARE REACHES 20.6% OF MERCHANDISE SALES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) posts fiscal 2025 results for the period ending 31st January 2026, with the stock up 29% since the start of 2026.\n\nE-commerce sales reach 20.6% of total merchandise sales, a record share, up from nearly 18% during the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020. In the fourth quarter, e-commerce represented 23.7% of merchandise sales.\n\nNon-merchandise sales rose 25% in the same quarter, driven by membership revenue which more than doubled year over year. Target operates as a general merchandise retailer with physical stores and digital platforms, blending everyday essentials with discounted differentiated merchandise to attract customers. Analysts note that opportunities in AI and other sectors may offer upside

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXPANDS BULLSEYE BUILDS WITH COMMUNITY PROGRAM ACROSS 13 CITIES BACKED BY $1 MILLION INVESTMENT

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) leads the Bullseye Builds with Community Program, a design-driven effort to transform community spaces across 13 U.S. cities. Backed by a $1 million investment, the rollout will officially kick off on 21st May 2026 in Denver and Las VegasThe program, launched in 2024, has completed 25 projects to date and has positively impacted over 250,000 community members with the help of Target team volunteers. In Denver, Target will partner with Trevista at Horace Mann Elementary School to upgrade the student wellness room and create outdoor learning spaces, featuring murals and new furniture. In Las Vegas, Target will team with The Just One Project to address food insecurity and enhance shopping areas and pantry facilities. Each project will involve more than 100 volunteers from TargetAdditional cities slated for this year’s Bullseye Builds include Austin, Birmingham, Columbus, Jersey City, Logan Township, Orlando, Washington, D.C.; Minneapolis-St. Paul; Phoenix; St. Louis; and Seattle. Kiera Fernandez, Executive Vice President and Chief Community and Stakeholder Engagement Officer, emphasized the importance of local insights in shaping these projects: "At Target, we believe investing in the places we call home builds strong communities and strong partnerships." The company also noted that team members contributed 1 million volunteer hours in the U.S. in 2025

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
