As the market continues to evolve, investors are advised to stay informed about various opportunities, including those in technology and retail.

+ TARGET UNVEILS AFFORDABLE WOMEN'S COLLECTION WITH PARKE AHEAD OF SUMMER

Target Corporation has announced the launch of a new women's collection in collaboration with fashion brand Parke, set to debut on April 25. This nearly 60-piece lineup will be available on Target.com and in select stores, with most items priced under $40, starting at just $5. The collection will include clothing, accessories, denim, leisurewear, and Parke's inaugural swimwear line, aiming to attract Gen Z shoppers and potentially boost foot traffic for Target.The upcoming launch of Target's women's collection in collaboration with Parke is a strategic move to capture the attention of Gen Z consumers, a demographic that is increasingly influential in the retail market. By offering a diverse range of affordable fashion items, Target aims to enhance its appeal among younger shoppers who prioritize both style and budget. This initiative is expected to drive foot traffic to stores and increase online sales, especially as the fashion retail landscape becomes more competitive.Established in 1962, Target Corporation is one of the largest discount retailers in the United States, operating nearly 2,000 stores and generating over $106 billion in sales for fiscal 2024. The company operates primarily in the Consumer Defensive sector, focusing on a broad assortment of merchandise, including apparel, beauty products, food, and home furnishings. With a market capitalization of approximately $58.95 billion, Target continues to be a significant player in the retail industry, leveraging its extensive physical store network to fulfill over 97% of its sales.While GF Value(TradeMark) data is not available for Target, the company's current P/E ratio of 16.01 suggests that it is trading at a relatively moderate valuation compared to its earnings. This P/E ratio aligns with the company's historical performance, indicating that investors may find the stock reasonably priced given its earnings potential. The GF Score(TradeMark) ranks stocks from 0 to 100 based on five key aspects: Financial Strength, Profitability, Growth, Valuation, and Momentum. Stocks with higher GF Score(TradeMark) values have been found to generate higher long-term returns.In the past three months, insider activity has shown a sell amount of $6.3 million, with no insider purchases reported. This trend may indicate a cautious sentiment among insiders regarding the company's near-term prospects. Overall, Target Corporation appears to be in a stable position with a GF Score(TradeMark) of 80/100, reflecting its potential for long-term returns. However, the insider selling activity and moderate growth rank suggest that investors should remain vigilant about the company's future performance.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: IKEA Expands U.S. Footprint with New Plan & Order Point in Newnan

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ3-B283-RRWB-W1D2-00000-00May 05, 2026: IKEA Expands U.S. Footprint with New Plan & Order Point in Newnan2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: IKEA Expands U.S. Footprint with New Plan & Order Point in Newnan

IKEA Group has announced the opening of its Newnan Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia. The location is set to open on the 22nd of April, 2026, and will feature a grand opening at 11 a.m. with exclusive tours, giveaways, food sampling, and more.

Located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, the new IKEA Newnan is designed to enhance customer accessibility to the brand. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, said the Newnan location is one of the many ways the company is expanding, adding, \"We are excited to open our doors and eagerly anticipate welcoming Newnan area residents to this new IKEA experience.\"

The Newnan Plan & Order Point offers planning assistance for home projects that require more help, such as kitchens and bathroom layouts. Customers meet with the IKEA team to plan and order home furnishings, but same-day pickup is not available; delivery or onsite free pickup can be arranged.

In addition to the new format, IKEA Group is investing in e-commerce capabilities, including new IKEA Family benefits, improved shipping and delivery options, Click & Collect services, and TaskRabbit assembly assistance to support both in-store and online shopping.

The expansion underscores IKEA's strategy to meet evolving consumer needs by adding accessible planning formats and convenient services as it grows its U.S. footprint.

INDEX

SECTION 1 IKEA GROUP PROFILE

SECTION 2 IKEA GROUP TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 IKEA GROUP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

Mission

IKEA Group's mission is to "create a better everyday life for the many people" by offering affordable, functional, and well-designed home furnishing solutions. Through strong partnerships with suppliers and franchisees, the group aligns its operations to bring accessible, high-quality living to communities worldwide.

Vision

The vision of IKEA Group is to "make a positive difference in people's lives" by fostering sustainability, from sourcing raw materials responsibly to enabling customers to live eco-friendly and sustainable lifestyles at home.

Values

IKEA Group is driven by eight core values that shape its culture and operations:

Togetherness: Fostering trust, teamwork, and joy in collaboration.

Caring for People and Planet: Serving as a catalyst for meaningful, lasting change.

Cost-Consciousness: Making beautiful and functional homes accessible to as many people as possible.

Simplicity: Embracing straightforward, pragmatic solutions and avoiding unnecessary bureaucracy.

Renew and Improve: Constantly seeking innovation and better approaches.

Different with a Meaning: Challenging the status quo, thinking unconventionally, and embracing mistakes as learning opportunities.

Give and Take Responsibility: Empowering people to grow through accountability.

Lead by Example: Encouraging leaders to inspire by action and embody the company's values.

With a global reach spanning 800 suppliers, 31 production units, and 25,000 co-workers, IKEA Group ensures its values resonate throughout its operations and impact millions of lives.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: Prince William travels to Yorkshire to meet with young farmers as pressure builds on industry
value.Overview: GB News, AGRICULTURE NEWS & UK NEWS, 758words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-NPF3-SFK4-T54C-00000-00Prince William travels to Yorkshire to meet with young farmers as pressure builds on industry2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisAGRICULTURE NEWS & UK NEWSPrince William travels to Yorkshire to meet with young farmers as pressure builds on industrySvar Nanan-Sen

Prince William travelled to North Yorkshire today to shine a spotlight on rural communities and their efforts towards environmental sustainability.

The Prince of Wales's engagements across the region are designed to highlight how countryside residents contribute to protecting natural landscapes whilst maintaining viable livelihoods.

The royal visit emphasises the crucial function these communities serve in caring for some of Britain's most cherished environments for generations to come.

Throughout the day, the heir to the throne is meeting with farmers and local people to gain insight into both the possibilities and difficulties confronting rural areas in the present day.

TRENDING

Stories

Videos

Your Say

The programme includes stops at a working farm in Swaledale and a community-owned renewable energy facility in the village of Bainbridge.

At a family farm in Swaledale, the prince spent time with agricultural workers from across the dale, exploring their experiences of running multi-generational farming operations.

William sat down with a group of young farmers, all parents themselves, to discuss how they are adapting to contemporary pressures on the industry.

The conversation centred on income diversification as a means of building long-term financial stability in an increasingly challenging sector.

Farmers present have pursued various supplementary revenue streams, from transforming traditional stone barns into holiday lets to establishing campsites that complement rather than detract from the surrounding landscape.

Participation in agri-environment schemes over extended periods has also provided additional income for those in attendance.

The Prince of Wales additionally met with the farmers' partners and children, learning how rural family life remains deeply connected to both work and the land.

Beyond food production, the farming community fulfils a significant environmental guardianship role across the region.

Sections of Swaledale and the adjacent uplands hold designations as Special Areas of Conservation and Special Protection Areas, recognising habitats and bird populations of international significance.

Local agricultural workers actively support nature recovery through traditional, environmentally conscious land management techniques that have been practised for generations.

These farmers are also capitalising on developing opportunities within nature finance markets to sustain their conservation efforts.

The protected landscapes and distinctive scenery draw considerable numbers of tourists, particularly during the summer months.

This visitor economy depends heavily on the farming community's continued stewardship of the terrain, demonstrating how agricultural and environmental interests can align in these rural settings.

Later in the afternoon, William is visiting Bainbridge, a picturesque village that hosts the River Bain Hydro plant.

The facility, owned by the local community, has been producing clean electricity since 2011 and supplies power to approximately 30 homes in the area.

An Archimedes screw mechanism sits visibly at the heart of the village, turning over the River Bain to generate energy.

The plant produces an average daily output of 224 kilowatt hours across the year, with surplus electricity fed into the National Grid.

Over its projected 40-year operational lifespan, the scheme is anticipated to prevent more than 3,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide emissions.

The National Park Authority's Sustainable Development Fund provided partial start-up funding for the project, which reduces reliance on fossil fuels whilst promoting local energy independence.

The hydroelectric scheme was engineered to operate harmoniously within its environment, avoiding disruption or damage to the natural landscape.

An additional benefit has emerged through reduced heavy goods vehicle traffic, with fewer oil tankers now required to deliver fuel supplies, easing pressure on local roads and bridges.

A complementary environmental initiative has blocked historic drainage channels across the surrounding moorland, trapping carbon within the ground.

This moorland restoration work has simultaneously created more consistent water flow to the hydro plant, improving its operational efficiency whilst lowering flood risk for Bainbridge and downstream communities.

During his visit, the prince is meeting community members responsible for maintaining the site and learning how this model of renewable energy generation could be replicated in other locations throughout Britain.

Our Standards:
The GB News Editorial Charter

value.Title: May 05, 2026: IKEA Group Unveils New Plan & Order Point in Newnan , GA

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-DBX3-RXH7-2439-00000-00May 05, 2026: IKEA Group Unveils New Plan & Order Point in Newnan, GA2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: IKEA Group Unveils New Plan & Order Point in Newnan, GA

IKEA Group has announced the opening of its new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia. This location is set to open its doors on the 22nd of April, 2026, and will feature a grand opening celebration at 11 a.m. with exclusive tours, giveaways, food sampling, and more.

Located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, the new IKEA Newnan is designed to enhance customer accessibility to the brand. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, expressed enthusiasm for the opening, stating, "The Newnan location is just one of the many ways we are expanding our business. We are excited to open our doors and eagerly anticipate welcoming Newnan area residents to this new IKEA experience."

The IKEA Newnan Plan & Order Point follows a new store format that aims to provide a more tailored shopping experience. Unlike traditional large-format IKEA stores, this location allows customers to meet with the IKEA team to plan and order home furnishing solutions that require additional assistance, such as kitchens and bathroom layouts. Although customers cannot take products home on the same day, IKEA staff can arrange delivery or facilitate free pick-ups from the convenient onsite location.

IKEA Group is committed to enhancing customer experience through a variety of meeting points and services that cater to diverse shopping preferences. In addition to the launch of new Plan & Order Points, IKEA U.S. has made substantial investments in e-commerce capabilities, including new IKEA Family benefits, improved shipping and delivery options, Click & Collect services, and TaskRabbit assembly assistance. These initiatives are designed to support both in-store and online shopping experiences, making it easier for customers to engage with the brand.

As IKEA Group continues to expand its presence in the U.S. market, the Newnan location signifies a strategic move to meet evolving consumer demands. By introducing innovative formats like the Plan & Order Point, IKEA aims to create more accessible shopping solutions, ultimately reinforcing its commitment to customer satisfaction and convenience.

INDEX

SECTION 1 IKEA GROUP PROFILE

SECTION 2 IKEA GROUP TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 IKEA GROUP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

Mission

IKEA Group's mission is to "create a better everyday life for the many people" by offering affordable, functional, and well-designed home furnishing solutions. Through strong partnerships with suppliers and franchisees, the group aligns its operations to bring accessible, high-quality living to communities worldwide.

Vision

The vision of IKEA Group is to "make a positive difference in people's lives" by fostering sustainability, from sourcing raw materials responsibly to enabling customers to live eco-friendly and sustainable lifestyles at home.

Values

IKEA Group is driven by eight core values that shape its culture and operations:

Togetherness: Fostering trust, teamwork, and joy in collaboration.

Caring for People and Planet: Serving as a catalyst for meaningful, lasting change.

Cost-Consciousness: Making beautiful and functional homes accessible to as many people as possible.

Simplicity: Embracing straightforward, pragmatic solutions and avoiding unnecessary bureaucracy.

Renew and Improve: Constantly seeking innovation and better approaches.

Different with a Meaning: Challenging the status quo, thinking unconventionally, and embracing mistakes as learning opportunities.

Give and Take Responsibility: Empowering people to grow through accountability.

Lead by Example: Encouraging leaders to inspire by action and embody the company's values.

With a global reach spanning 800 suppliers, 31 production units, and 25,000 co-workers, IKEA Group ensures its values resonate throughout its operations and impact millions of lives.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: The Battery Show South 2026 Powers the Future of Energy Storage and Innovation

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-NYV3-S5D5-Y37X-00000-00The Battery Show South 2026 Powers the Future of Energy Storage and Innovation2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisThe Battery Show South 2026 Powers the Future of Energy Storage and Innovation

INFORMA PLC ("IFJPY,IFPJF-0") - The Battery Show South 2026 Powers the Future of Energy - Storage and Innovation The Battery Show South and Electric &Hybrid Vehicle Expo, the Southeasts premier event for advanced battery technology and energy storage solutions, concluded its highly anticipated gathering at the Charlotte Convention Center on April 22-23, 2026, leaving attendees inspired by the latest innovations in energy storage, electric vehicles (EVs), and renewable energy technologies. This years event brought together a dynamic lineup of speakers and panelists, making it a hub for collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and technological advancement. The Battery Show South 2026 showcased the cutting-edge solutions driving the future of energy storage and sustainable power systems. Insightful Keynote Presentations The Battery Show South 2026 featured three thought-provoking keynote sessions from industry leaders including Christopher Chung, CEO, Economic Development Partnership of North Carolina, with an in-depth look at the states significance in The Battery Belt; Brad Li, President of the Americas, Sineng Americas, examining how AI is reshaping the global economy, and the energy systems that power it; and Ryan Melsert, CEO &CTO, American Battery Technology Company, exploring the battery supply chain.

Comprehensive Conference Programming The Battery Show South Conference delivered two days of expert-led learning designed to equip industry leaders with the knowledge and tools to stay ahead in the rapidly evolving battery, energy storage, and EV industry. Attendees were able to view technical sessions &case studies with in-depth analysis; roundtables that kicked off each day with a notable keynote address followed by a panel of top industry leaders discussing the most important market disruptors; and Lightning Talks, 15 minute sessions providing an overview of emerging technologies, new research, and creative solutions from across the battery, energy storage, and EV landscape. The Battery Show South 2026 has solidified its position as the Southeasts leading platform for battery technology and electric vehicle innovation, says Shamara Ray, Group Event Director, Manufacturing by Informa. This years event not only showcased groundbreaking technologies but also fostered impactful connections that will drive the future of energy storage and sustainable power systems. We are witnessing the continued evolution of this dynamic industry. Networking and Collaboration The event provided unparalleled networking opportunities, connecting professionals from across the energy and manufacturing ecosystems. From informal meetups to structured matchmaking sessions, attendees forged valuable connections and partnerships to drive innovation in the energy sector. Co-Located Synergies with MD&M South 2026 For the first time, The Battery Show South was co-located with MD&M South, creating a unique opportunity for attendees to explore synergies across battery, EV, energy storage and advanced manufacturing technologies. This collaboration allowed participants to access both events with a single pass, broadening their exposure to cutting-edge innovations. The Battery Show South returns to the Georgia World Congress Center in Atlanta, GA, on April 21-22, 2027. Learn more about the show here. About The Battery Show The Battery Show is the largest and most comprehensive advanced battery technology event, co-located with Electric &Hybrid Vehicle Technology Expo, the only trade show and conference exclusively dedicated to advanced battery and the electric and hybrid vehicle drivetrain. Visitors to our shows can discover and demo the latest products, technology, and solutions from nearly a thousand suppliers, network with tens of thousands of attendees and access a breadth of education across multiple tracks and technical sessions. The Battery Show is organized by Informa Markets Engineering and includes The Battery Show Europe, The Battery Show North America, The Battery Show South, The Battery Show India and The Battery Show Asia. Listed media partners include Informa Market's Battery Technology and DesignNews. For more information, please visit informamarkets.com. About Manufacturing by Informa Manufacturing by Informa is the leading B2B event producer, publisher, and digital media business for the world's $3-trillion advanced, technology-based manufacturing industry. Our print and electronic products deliver trusted information to the engineering market and leverage our proprietary 1.3-million-name database to connect suppliers with buyers and purchase influencers. We produce more than 50 events and conferences in a dozen countries, connecting manufacturing professionals from around the globe. The Manufacturing portfolio is organized by Informa Markets, a subsidiary of Informa plc (LON:INF), the world's leading exhibitions organizer that brings a diverse range of specialist markets to life, unlocking opportunities and helping them to thrive 365 days of the year. For more information, please visit www.informamarkets.com. Media Contact Manufacturing by Informapr.ime@informa.com

value.Title: May 07, 2026: Joint Initiative by Southwire and SKILLED Nation Bolsters Local Workforce Through Scholarships

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-3F43-SHSP-W0B2-00000-00May 07, 2026: Joint Initiative by Southwire and SKILLED Nation Bolsters Local Workforce Through Scholarships2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:01ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: Joint Initiative by Southwire and SKILLED Nation Bolsters Local Workforce Through Scholarships

On 07th May 2026, Southwire and SKILLED Nation announced an expansion of their 12 for Life initiative to fund more skilled-trades education.

The program has disbursed over $200,000 to students across Georgia and Alabama, and the latest round adds $100,000 in new scholarships this year.

First recipients include Magnum Cannon, a graduate studying Industrial Systems Technology and HVAC at Northwest Shoals Community College who said, "Receiving this scholarship means a lot to me." Robert Anthony, studying at West Georgia Technical College, added that the funding relieved financial burdens and let him focus on his studies.

Burt Fealing, Executive Vice President at Southwire, commented, "These scholarships open doors to meaningful careers and lasting achievement." The joint effort underscores how technology, training, and local partnerships can support a strong skilled-labor economy.

INDEX

SECTION 1 SOUTHWIRE PROFILE

SECTION 2 SOUTHWIRE TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 3 SOUTHWIRE RANKING

SECTION 1 SOUTHWIRE PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Southwire Company, founded in 1950 by Roy Richards, is a leading manufacturer of wire and cable products, headquartered in Carrollton, Georgia. With over 8,500 employees across 38 facilities, Southwire serves construction, utility, and industrial markets, producing electrical cables, copper rods, and renewable energy solutions. Its 2023 revenue exceeded $8 billion. Southwires focus on innovation includes advanced conductor technologies and sustainable manufacturing, with initiatives like carbon-neutral goals by 2050. Acquisitions like Coleman Cable have expanded its portfolio. Competing with Prysmian and Nexans, Southwire emphasizes quality, safety, and community engagement through the Richards Family Foundation.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-JTR3-RSBB-S0HD-00000-00IKEA GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA OPENS NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN TO ENHANCE CUSTOMIZED HOME SOLUTIONS

IKEA Group announced a new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia, set to open on the 22nd of April, 2026, with an 11 a.m. grand opening.The store is located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, and aims to improve access for Newnan residents. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, noted that the location reflects IKEA's ongoing expansion as part of its U.S. growth strategy.The Plan & Order Point offers planning help for items such as kitchens and bathrooms, with staff available to design and place orders. While customers cannot take products home the same day, delivery or free onsite pickup can be arranged.IKEA Group is expanding U.S. capabilities through investments in e-commerce, new IKEA Family benefits, improved shipping and delivery, Click & Collect, and TaskRabbit assembly assistance to support both in-store and online experiences.

+ IKEA'S AI TOOLKIT TRANSFORMS DESIGN, DELIVERY AND CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE

IKEA Group is pursuing an AI driven shift to an omni-channel model, with digital channels now about 30 percent of its business. During a visit to India on the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, explained how AI investments are reshaping customer experiences and operations.The strategy includes a Bengaluru digital hub and the Locus acquisition to boost last mile delivery, strengthening IKEA's presence in a fast growing app driven market.Internal tools such as AI based design software cut room planning time from hours to minutes, while the IKEA Kreativ platform helps customers visualize products at varied price points.The company frames these efforts as part of a sustainable growth plan with ethical AI guiding expansions in India and beyond.

+ VANTAA IKEA PILOT OFFERS PRE-BOOKED ASSISTANCE AND APP-GUIDED SHOPPING

IKEA Group is piloting an accessibility initiative at the Vantaa store in Finland, which began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping with NaviLens navigation to support customers with disabilities. The two main offerings are a 90-minute assisted-shopping session booked in advance with a trained coworker and the NaviLens app that provides audio guidance when shoppers scan codes around the store. Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland, says, "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs." The pilot is described as a learning platform to collect feedback and refine services for potential rollout elsewhere, reinforcing IKEA's commitment to inclusive journeys online and in-store with support from the Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise and international accessibility efforts.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) HARNESSES APP METRICS AND CREATOR PORTAL TO DRIVE TARGETED MARKETING

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is reshaping its marketing by merging technology, data analytics and influencer partnerships, according to Molly Battin on 6th May 2026. The retailer uses first-party data and regional weather signals to tailor recommendations in real time, while The Home Depot app drives higher spend—three to four times more than non-users—helping younger customers navigate stores through guided experiences. Store Mode assists in navigating the warehouse aisles, while the company’s creator portal expands partnerships with influencers who resonate with DIY communities. The Home Depot prioritizes creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values to customers. Campaigns tied to these efforts have supported new customer acquisition in niche categories, highlighting a broader shift in the CMO role toward technologically driven, financially accountable marketing and closer integration of digital tools with influencer networks.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) PRO ECOSYSTEM PROGRESS SUPPORTS ELEVATED VALUATIONS

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, several firms uplifted price targets into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism around Q4 results and the PRO pushGuggenheim and Wells Fargo highlighted progress in the PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings based on the companys strategic investments and long-term growth trajectorySome analysts remained cautious about the near-term, with RBC Capital and Bernstein assigning neutral views and lower targets. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets slightly amid concerns about remodeling demand and its impact on revenueHome Depot reaffirmed a 2026 total sales growth target of about 2.5% to 4.5% and rolled out PRO enhancements, including an expanded digital workspace for pros and a real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value remains US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions near 4.19% and some margin and P/E multiple adjustments as investors evaluate the PRO initiatives

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) REPORTS OPENING OF SAN MARCOS STORE BACKED BY $13 MILLION INVESTMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) is expanding into San Marcos with a $13 million store project confirmed on 28th April 2026. This will be the retailer’s first store in the college town, which has seen rapid growth in housing and retail. Plans filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) indicate a 135,918-square-foot footprint, including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction scheduled to start in September 2026 and to finish by August 2027. The new location sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and slated to open in October 2026. San Marcos, on the I-35 corridor and home to Texas State University, continues to attract development, aligning with Home Depot to broaden its presence. The company noted a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations nationwide

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PARTNERS WITH MADE IN TO LAUNCH EXCLUSIVE CAST IRON LINE

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) partners with Made In to launch an exclusive cast iron collection at Williams-Sonoma stores on 11th May 2026, with direct-to-consumer availability on Made In's site by June 1st. Williams-Sonoma will also stock Made In's American-made stainless steel collection, expanding its stainless cookware assortment. Made In, founded in 2017, counts more than 2,000 restaurant customers and saw revenue grow 52% from 2024 to 2025, with a large portion of sales in the fourth quarter. The cast iron line has been refined to be lighter while preserving performance, reflecting the brand's focus on usability. Long-running discussions with Williams-Sonoma culminated in a differentiated product launch. Made In also distributes through Crate & Barrel, Bloomingdale's and Ace Hardware, and plans a European fulfillment center by September as part of its international rollout.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) GOLDMAN SACHS BOOSTS RATING TO BUY WITH HIGHER PRICE TARGET

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a constructive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.The investment bank projects Williams-Sonoma to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates, while non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast at $13.00, down 33.82%. The update aligns with a broader trend of reassessing the home goods sector as consumer spending evolves.Investors will monitor how Williams-Sonoma translates this outlook into stronger revenue growth and margin discipline in the coming year.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) NAMES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO HEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined in 2003 as a Pottery Barn district manager and rose through the Rejuvenation banner to become senior vice president in 2020.In her new role, she will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives to drive Rejuvenation within the overall brand portfolio.CEO Laura Alber noted that Taylor's leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to reach billion-dollar potential, reflecting her track record and design-oriented approach.Rejuvenation has expanded with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has partnered on a Roll & Hill lighting collection. Williams-Sonoma has named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The firm projects 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ CONSUMERS ELIGIBLE FOR VOUCHERS IN FIBERGLASS MATTRESS SETTLEMENT BY ASHLEY FURNITURE AND RESIDENT HOME

Ashley Furniture Industries LLC and Resident Home LLC are notifying a $9 million class action settlement over certain mattresses sold between October 1, 2017 and June 30, 2024. The claims center on fiberglass used as a fire retardant material that could escape and pose harm, with eligible buyers set to receive vouchers. Eligible consumers are those who purchased affected mattresses designed, manufactured, or sold by the two companies during the period. The settlement aims to address claims of economic loss, property damage, and physical harm associated with the products. Claim deadline is July 17, 2026. Vouchers will be distributed on a pro rata basis, calculated by dividing the total settlement fund by the number of valid claims. Vouchers are redeemable on AshleyFurniture.com and NectarSleep.com, and any funds remaining after distribution may be issued as cash payments. Claimants must provide documentation. If a claimant received a unique ID and PIN, they may complete only part of the form; others must provide proof of purchase such as receipts, store records, or photos of mattress labels. The final approval hearing is scheduled for September 24, 2026, after which vouchers will be issued following any appeals.

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE REFRAMES BRAND WITH LUXE LINE TO ENTER HIGHER PRICE POINTS

Ashley is moving into upscale with Luxe, which was unveiled May 1, 2026. The initiative represents a notable shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores and has historically centered its assortments on value-driven midrange products.Luxe is a full-line offering including living, dining, and bedroom furniture. It will be available in about 70% of Ashley's stores, via enterprise and licensee partners as well as online. Price points position Luxe above budget lines but below luxury brands, with sofas typically $1,000–$1,200 and many sectionals from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, described Luxe as a way to reach new customers and differentiate the brand in a crowded market.The line leverages Ashley's existing supply chain, mixing domestic manufacturing with imports to preserve pricing and quality. Stores will feature dedicated Luxe areas with custom signage, and the Las Vegas flagship will showcase the line as a focal point. A campaign with Hailee Steinfeld supports the launch, and Todd Wanek calls Luxe an evolution that remains within reach.

+ SPANAWAY HUB PURCHASE SIGNALS AMAZON'S GROWING E-COMMERCE LOGISTICS PUSH

Amazon has bought Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—about 1.1 million square feet along with nearby parcels—for more than $200 million, as reported on the 27th of April 2026.The move strengthens Amazon's logistics network in the Pacific Northwest, aiding faster deliveries as e-commerce grows.Ashley Furniture is divesting the property as part of a strategic realignment, leaving questions about its future distribution plans.The sale spotlights the brisk commercial real estate activity in the Pacific Northwest and Amazon's continued push into large-scale warehousing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) LAUNCHES MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD FOR CONTRACTORS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card with Synchrony for professional contractors, usable anywhere American Express is accepted, as announced on the 6th of May 2026. Built to support a contractor-focused strategy, the card extends payment options beyond Lowe's stores to deepen relationships with pro buyers. The company projects 2029 revenue of $100.6 billion and earnings of $8.1 billion, with revenue growth of 5.3% annually. Near term, analysts expect limited earnings impact as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides a 2026 comp outlook of flat to low single digits

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORES ACROSS GROWTH MARKETS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Expands Footprint With New Stores Across Growth MarketsLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space and a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, with more than 100 associates on staff. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will showcase redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms that blend style, affordability and innovation. New showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Other openings are planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. The expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet growing demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. District Manager Christian Redman highlighted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is positioned to deliver value, convenience and service to the community. Investors will assess how these locations contribute to sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) OPENS 94,000-SQ.-FT PORT ST. LUCIE STORE AS PART OF GROWTH PLAN

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) unveils expansion plans including a Port St. Lucie location in Florida sized at about 94,000 square feet with a 30,000-square-foot garden center and more than 100 staff, slated to open in early June 2026. The company also plans four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky to begin operations later this year.The new stores will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for quick paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup to enhance convenience for customers.Lowe's operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, and handles roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales exceeded $83 billion. The MyLowe's Rewards program provides points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, reinforcing customer loyalty

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXTENDS HEMP-DERIVED THC BEVERAGE PILOT TO THREE ADDITIONAL STATES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) expands its hemp-derived THC beverage pilot beyond Minnesota to Florida, Illinois and Texas, effective May 11, 2026, subject to local regulations. The Minnesota launch laid the groundwork for a broader geographic test of product availability and guest interest.Brands Cann, Wynk, Trail Magic, Stigma, Gigli and Daizy’s will be available across stores in the three new states, complementing the existing Minnesota rollout.A Target spokesperson states the retailer is "always exploring new ways to meet our guests’ evolving preferences, grounded in our merchandising authority and focus on thoughtfully curating a relevant assortment." This expansion aims to measure consumer response and supply-chain readiness amid a regulatory landscape that could shift with federal action on hemp-derived products, a market currently estimated at $28 billion

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) REPLACES AFFILIATE MODEL WITH DUAL CREATOR INITIATIVES TO DRIVE DIGITAL ENGAGEMENT

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is restructuring its social-marketing framework by moving to two creator programs, Club Target and Target Ambassadors, effective 10th May 2026, replacing the previous affiliate program. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) notes that the approach aligns rewards more closely with engagement and sales, with Ambassadors focusing on high-reach voices and Club Target helping everyday shoppers participate as creators. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) also highlights the broader shift of shopping into the digital environment, where peers like Walmart and Amazon are actively investing

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) SETS PATH TO RECOVERY WITH PARKE COLLABORATION, RBC CAPITAL RAISES TARGET TO $132

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Posts early signs of progress in its turnaround as RBC Capital reports a higher price target of $132 from $130 and an Outperform rating ahead of the Q1 results on 9th May 2026On April 21, Target rolled out a limited-time collaboration with Parke, a premium brand for Gen Z shoppers, through its community-driven approach that aims to reflect consumer preferences in product developmentTarget remains a general merchandise retailer delivering everyday essentials and differentiated products via stores and digital channels with a focus on value for its guestsRBC notes that while Target has momentum, some AI stocks offer greater upside potential with lower risk, underscoring a broader investment landscape for investors

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

IKEA Group is pursuing an AI driven shift to an omni-channel model, with digital channels now about 30 percent of its business. During a visit to India on the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, explained how AI investments are reshaping customer experiences and operations.The strategy includes a Bengaluru digital hub and the Locus acquisition to boost last mile delivery, strengthening IKEA's presence in a fast growing app driven market.Internal tools such as AI based design software cut room planning time from hours to minutes, while the IKEA Kreativ platform helps customers visualize products at varied price points.The company frames these efforts as part of a sustainable growth plan with ethical AI guiding expansions in India and beyond.

+ VANTAA IKEA PILOT OFFERS PRE-BOOKED ASSISTANCE AND APP-GUIDED SHOPPING

IKEA Group is piloting an accessibility initiative at the Vantaa store in Finland, which began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping with NaviLens navigation to support customers with disabilities. The two main offerings are a 90-minute assisted-shopping session booked in advance with a trained coworker and the NaviLens app that provides audio guidance when shoppers scan codes around the store. Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland, says, "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs." The pilot is described as a learning platform to collect feedback and refine services for potential rollout elsewhere, reinforcing IKEA's commitment to inclusive journeys online and in-store with support from the Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise and international accessibility efforts.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) HARNESSES APP METRICS AND CREATOR PORTAL TO DRIVE TARGETED MARKETING

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is reshaping its marketing by merging technology, data analytics and influencer partnerships, according to Molly Battin on 6th May 2026. The retailer uses first-party data and regional weather signals to tailor recommendations in real time, while The Home Depot app drives higher spend—three to four times more than non-users—helping younger customers navigate stores through guided experiences. Store Mode assists in navigating the warehouse aisles, while the company’s creator portal expands partnerships with influencers who resonate with DIY communities. The Home Depot prioritizes creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values to customers. Campaigns tied to these efforts have supported new customer acquisition in niche categories, highlighting a broader shift in the CMO role toward technologically driven, financially accountable marketing and closer integration of digital tools with influencer networks.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) PRO ECOSYSTEM PROGRESS SUPPORTS ELEVATED VALUATIONS

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, several firms uplifted price targets into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism around Q4 results and the PRO pushGuggenheim and Wells Fargo highlighted progress in the PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings based on the companys strategic investments and long-term growth trajectorySome analysts remained cautious about the near-term, with RBC Capital and Bernstein assigning neutral views and lower targets. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets slightly amid concerns about remodeling demand and its impact on revenueHome Depot reaffirmed a 2026 total sales growth target of about 2.5% to 4.5% and rolled out PRO enhancements, including an expanded digital workspace for pros and a real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value remains US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions near 4.19% and some margin and P/E multiple adjustments as investors evaluate the PRO initiatives

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) REPORTS OPENING OF SAN MARCOS STORE BACKED BY $13 MILLION INVESTMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) is expanding into San Marcos with a $13 million store project confirmed on 28th April 2026. This will be the retailer’s first store in the college town, which has seen rapid growth in housing and retail. Plans filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) indicate a 135,918-square-foot footprint, including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction scheduled to start in September 2026 and to finish by August 2027. The new location sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and slated to open in October 2026. San Marcos, on the I-35 corridor and home to Texas State University, continues to attract development, aligning with Home Depot to broaden its presence. The company noted a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations nationwide

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) NAMES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO HEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined in 2003 as a Pottery Barn district manager and rose through the Rejuvenation banner to become senior vice president in 2020.In her new role, she will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives to drive Rejuvenation within the overall brand portfolio.CEO Laura Alber noted that Taylor's leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to reach billion-dollar potential, reflecting her track record and design-oriented approach.Rejuvenation has expanded with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has partnered on a Roll & Hill lighting collection. Williams-Sonoma has named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The firm projects 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) EVERCORE SETS $200 TARGET AND KEEPS HOLD RATING

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) maintains a Hold rating as Evercore raises its price target to $200 from $195 in a 14th April 2026 report. Analyst Oliver Wintermantel, who leads Williams-Sonoma coverage at Evercore, has a 66.7% success rate and a 23.9% average return over the past year, according to TipRanks. The target adjustment reflects Evercore’s view of market dynamics and Williams-Sonoma’s operational strategies within a competitive home-goods landscape. While the rating remains Hold, the new target suggests investors should monitor seasonal trends, inventory management and supply-chain efficiency as Williams-Sonoma pursues its projected performance.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) GOLDMAN SACHS BOOSTS RATING TO BUY WITH HIGHER PRICE TARGET

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a constructive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.The investment bank projects Williams-Sonoma to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates, while non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast at $13.00, down 33.82%. The update aligns with a broader trend of reassessing the home goods sector as consumer spending evolves.Investors will monitor how Williams-Sonoma translates this outlook into stronger revenue growth and margin discipline in the coming year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE REFRAMES BRAND WITH LUXE LINE TO ENTER HIGHER PRICE POINTS

Ashley is moving into upscale with Luxe, which was unveiled May 1, 2026. The initiative represents a notable shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores and has historically centered its assortments on value-driven midrange products.Luxe is a full-line offering including living, dining, and bedroom furniture. It will be available in about 70% of Ashley's stores, via enterprise and licensee partners as well as online. Price points position Luxe above budget lines but below luxury brands, with sofas typically $1,000–$1,200 and many sectionals from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, described Luxe as a way to reach new customers and differentiate the brand in a crowded market.The line leverages Ashley's existing supply chain, mixing domestic manufacturing with imports to preserve pricing and quality. Stores will feature dedicated Luxe areas with custom signage, and the Las Vegas flagship will showcase the line as a focal point. A campaign with Hailee Steinfeld supports the launch, and Todd Wanek calls Luxe an evolution that remains within reach.

+ FRONTLINEIQ DEBUTS INDUSTRY-LEADING AI COACHING FOR IN-PERSON SALES AT HIGH POINT

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the AI sales coaching platform for in-person teams, announced the Ashley AI Advantage, developed with Ashley Furniture Industries, at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026.The program introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time conversation coaching for frontline associates, enabling practice with real customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven selling methods.The Ashley AI Advantage is free for Ashley Wholesale customers and licensees, with scalable options. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, called it transformative, while Ashley's John Mask said it offers a new level of showroom-floor confidence and consistency.As a turnkey entry point pre-configured for home furnishings, the platform can be customized for retailers' training processes. More information is available at www.frontlineiq.ai/ashley.

+ SPANAWAY HUB PURCHASE SIGNALS AMAZON'S GROWING E-COMMERCE LOGISTICS PUSH

Amazon has bought Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—about 1.1 million square feet along with nearby parcels—for more than $200 million, as reported on the 27th of April 2026.The move strengthens Amazon's logistics network in the Pacific Northwest, aiding faster deliveries as e-commerce grows.Ashley Furniture is divesting the property as part of a strategic realignment, leaving questions about its future distribution plans.The sale spotlights the brisk commercial real estate activity in the Pacific Northwest and Amazon's continued push into large-scale warehousing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) LAUNCHES MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD FOR CONTRACTORS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card with Synchrony for professional contractors, usable anywhere American Express is accepted, as announced on the 6th of May 2026. Built to support a contractor-focused strategy, the card extends payment options beyond Lowe's stores to deepen relationships with pro buyers. The company projects 2029 revenue of $100.6 billion and earnings of $8.1 billion, with revenue growth of 5.3% annually. Near term, analysts expect limited earnings impact as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides a 2026 comp outlook of flat to low single digits

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORES ACROSS GROWTH MARKETS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Expands Footprint With New Stores Across Growth MarketsLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space and a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, with more than 100 associates on staff. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will showcase redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms that blend style, affordability and innovation. New showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Other openings are planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. The expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet growing demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. District Manager Christian Redman highlighted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is positioned to deliver value, convenience and service to the community. Investors will assess how these locations contribute to sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) OPENS 94,000-SQ.-FT PORT ST. LUCIE STORE AS PART OF GROWTH PLAN

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) unveils expansion plans including a Port St. Lucie location in Florida sized at about 94,000 square feet with a 30,000-square-foot garden center and more than 100 staff, slated to open in early June 2026. The company also plans four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky to begin operations later this year.The new stores will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for quick paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup to enhance convenience for customers.Lowe's operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, and handles roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales exceeded $83 billion. The MyLowe's Rewards program provides points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, reinforcing customer loyalty

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) BETS ON SPEED WITH NATIONWIDE NEXT-DAY DELIVERY EXPANSION AFTER 2025 SALES DECLINE

As Of The 28th Of April 2026, Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Expands Next-Day Delivery To 60% Of The U.S. Population, Reaching Over 50 Metro Areas, With Many Deliveries FreeThe Move Emphasizes Speed In Online Shopping And Supports Target's Efforts To Rebuild Shopper TrustEligible Items Rose To 85% Of All In-Store Selections, With Orders Placed By 6 P.M. Local Time Arriving The Next Day; Standard Delivery Is $5.99, And Orders Over $35 Qualify For Free DeliveryThe Initiative Is Part Of The New Chapter $5 Billion Strategy Led By Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke, Following A 1.7% Net Sales Decline In 2025, And Includes Store Refreshes And New Collaborations

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) NON-MERCHANDISE REVENUE SURGES 25% IN QUARTER

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) posts fiscal 2025 results for the period ending 31st January 2026, with stock up 29% since the start of 2026. E-commerce sales reach 20.6% of total merchandise sales, and the fourth quarter e-commerce share rose to 23.7%. Non-merchandise sales rose 25% in the quarter, with membership revenue more than doubling year over year. Target operates as a general merchandise retailer with stores and digital platforms, delivering a mix of everyday essentials and differentiated merchandise to customers. Analysts note that opportunities in AI and other sectors may offer upside as market dynamics evolve

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) PARTNERS WITH LOCAL NONPROFITS TO REVITALIZE COMMUNITY SPACES ACROSS 13 CITIES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) expands its Bullseye Builds with Community Program, a design-driven effort to revitalize community spaces in 13 U.S. cities with a $1 million investment. The initiative will begin on 21st May 2026 in Denver and Las VegasLaunched in 2024, the program has completed 25 projects to date and supported more than 250,000 community members with help from Target volunteers. In Denver, Trevista at Horace Mann Elementary School will upgrade the student wellness room and create outdoor learning spaces; In Las Vegas, The Just One Project will address food insecurity and improve shopping areas and pantry facilities. Each project will involve more than 100 volunteers from TargetOther cities include Austin, Birmingham, Columbus, Jersey City, Logan Township, Orlando, Washington, D.C., Minneapolis-St. Paul, Phoenix, St. Louis, and Seattle. "At Target, we believe investing in the places we call home builds strong communities and strong partnerships," said Kiera Fernandez, Executive Vice President and Chief Community and Stakeholder Engagement Officer. The company also notes that team members contributed 1 million volunteer hours in the U.S. in 2025

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JK3-3CP3-SCNX-335F-00000-00Rotary Club of Lake Chatuge welcomed Ward2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. 4BRotary Club of Lake Chatuge welcomed WardGUEST WRITERRON WOOD

Tony Ward, Habitat Restoration Coordinator with MountainTrue recently visited the Rotary Club of Lake Chatuge and shared an overview of the organiza- tion’s expanding environmental work across western North Carolina and north Georgia.

Ward described the nonprofit’s mission as “championing resilient forests, clean water and healthy communities in the southern Blue Ridge Mountains.” and highlighted projects focused on clean water, forest restoration, invasive species control and sustainable community development. MountainTrue currently serves a 28-county region that includes much of western North Carolina as well as Towns and Union counties in north Georgia.

The organization’s work ranges from environmental advocacy and policy initiatives to hands-on restoration projects. Ward emphasized that MountainTrue supports “smart development” and responsible land management practices rather than opposing development outright. Among the organization’s major focuses is water quality monitoring. Through partnerships with Georgia Adopt-A-Stream, volunteers collect monthly water samples throughout the region to test for water chemistry, turbidity, E. coli levels and aquatic insect populations that help indicate stream health. MountainTrue also operates a seasonal Swim Guide program from Memorial Day through Labor Day, conducting weekly testing at popular swimming locations including Lake Chatuge, Lake Nottely, the Hiwassee River and Valley River.

Forest restoration and invasive species control make up another major component of MountainTrue’s work. Ward said crews routinely remove invasive plants from public lands, parks, greenways and river corridors throughout the region. Ward highlighted several habitat restoration projects, including streambank stabilization work using “live staking,” a process that uses cuttings from native plants to stabilize eroding streambanks naturally.

The nonprofit has also played a major role in combating invasive aquatic species, particularly the spread of parrot feather, an aggressive aquatic plant that once threatened coves in Lake Chatuge. “We were able to rally some agencies together and with a lot of support from the local community, we were able to get parrot feather under control in Lake Chatuge,” Ward said.

The organization also hosts the annual Lake Chatuge Shoreline Cleanup each November, drawing volunteers from across the area to remove litter and debris from shorelines and waterways. Additional initiatives include assisting with trail planning, greenway development, affordable workforce housing advocacy, transportation planning and renewable energy programs.

MountainTrue receives approximately 96 percent of its funding through private donations and volunteer support. Ward encouraged community members to become involved by participating in water monitoring, invasive species removal, tree planting projects, or advocacy efforts.

Tony Ward

value.Title: May 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Advance Workforce Development with 12 for Life Scholarships

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-2XJ3-S207-D1YF-00000-00May 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Advance Workforce Development with 12 for Life Scholarships2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:01ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Advance Workforce Development with 12 for Life Scholarships

On 07th May 2026, Southwire and SKILLED Nation expanded their scholarship program under the 12 for Life initiative, signaling a deeper commitment to developing the next generation of skilled trades talent.

Since the program began, more than $200,000 has been disbursed to students across Georgia and Alabama, and the latest round adds $100,000 in scholarships this year, highlighting the ongoing investment in local communities.

Among the first recipients are Magnum Cannon, a 12 for Life alumnus studying Industrial Systems Technology and HVAC at Northwest Shoals Community College, and Robert Anthony, who is pursuing studies at West Georgia Technical College. Cannon said, "Receiving this scholarship means a lot to me," while Anthony noted that the support helped him concentrate on his coursework. Burt Fealing, Executive Vice President at Southwire, added, "These scholarships open doors to meaningful careers and lasting achievement." The collaboration aims to strengthen the regional economy by expanding access to skilled labor.

INDEX

SECTION 1 SOUTHWIRE PROFILE

SECTION 2 SOUTHWIRE TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 3 SOUTHWIRE RANKING

SECTION 1 SOUTHWIRE PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Southwire Company, founded in 1950 by Roy Richards, is a leading manufacturer of wire and cable products, headquartered in Carrollton, Georgia. With over 8,500 employees across 38 facilities, Southwire serves construction, utility, and industrial markets, producing electrical cables, copper rods, and renewable energy solutions. Its 2023 revenue exceeded $8 billion. Southwires focus on innovation includes advanced conductor technologies and sustainable manufacturing, with initiatives like carbon-neutral goals by 2050. Acquisitions like Coleman Cable have expanded its portfolio. Competing with Prysmian and Nexans, Southwire emphasizes quality, safety, and community engagement through the Richards Family Foundation.

value.Title: May 07, 2026: Southwire, SKILLED Nation Partner to Fund Trades Education and Careers

urn:contentItem:6JK7-7K23-S048-H1VB-00000-00May 07, 2026: Southwire, SKILLED Nation Partner to Fund Trades Education and Careers2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:01ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: Southwire, SKILLED Nation Partner to Fund Trades Education and Careers

On 07th May 2026, Southwire and SKILLED Nation expanded their scholarship program as part of the 12 for Life initiative, a move aimed at nurturing the next generation of skilled tradespeople. Since its inception, the program has disbursed over $200,000 to students across Georgia and Alabama.

The Southwire 12 for Life SKILLED Scholarship was established two years ago to help graduates pursue community college, technical, or vocational education.

The latest round adds $100,000 in scholarships this year, reinforcing Southwire's commitment to local communities and their futures.

Among the first recipients is Magnum Cannon, who is studying Industrial Systems Technology and HVAC at Northwest Shoals Community College; he said, "Receiving this scholarship means a lot to me." Another recipient, Robert Anthony, noted that the funding relieved financial burdens and allowed him to concentrate on his studies at West Georgia Technical College.

Burt Fealing, Executive Vice President at Southwire, said, "These scholarships open doors to meaningful careers and lasting achievement." The partnership continues to expand access to skilled trades and strengthen the local economy by meeting workforce needs in the communities it serves.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-3NN3-RSP0-R4F1-00000-00May 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Forge Pathways for Future Talent2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Forge Pathways for Future Talent

Southwire and SKILLED Nation are making significant strides in supporting the next generation of skilled tradespeople. The partnership aims to create scholarship opportunities for high school graduates looking to continue their education. Since their collaboration began, Southwire has committed to enhancing educational pathways through the 12 for Life® initiative, which has seen tangible benefits for students aspiring to enter the workforce in critical fields.

On 07th May 2026, the two organizations announced an expansion of their scholarship program, which has already disbursed over $200,000 in funding to students across Georgia and Alabama. The Southwire 12 for Life SKILLED Scholarship was established two years ago and has successfully aided students in pursuing community college, technical, or vocational education. The latest round will provide an additional $100,000 in scholarships this year, underscoring Southwire's dedication to investing in local communities and their futures.

Among the first recipients was Magnum Cannon, a graduate of the 12 for Life program, who emphasized the scholarship's impact on his educational journey. "Receiving this scholarship means a lot to me," said Cannon, who is currently studying Industrial Systems Technology and HVAC at Northwest Shoals Community College. His experiences illustrate the program's goal of equipping students with practical skills necessary for success in high-demand careers.

Another recipient, Robert Anthony, echoed similar sentiments, noting that the scholarship alleviated financial burdens and allowed him to concentrate on his studies at West Georgia Technical College. The personal stories of these students highlight the broader mission of Southwire to foster meaningful career pathways in skilled trades, reinforcing the importance of technical education in today’s economy.

Burt Fealing, Executive Vice President at Southwire, flagged the significance of this initiative, stating, "These scholarships open doors to meaningful careers and lasting achievement." As Southwire continues to expand its partnership with SKILLED Nation, the company remains committed to nurturing talent and addressing workforce needs in the communities it serves. This collaborative effort not only benefits individual students but also strengthens the local economy by promoting skilled labor.

INDEX

SECTION 2 SOUTHWIRE TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 3 SOUTHWIRE RANKING

SECTION 1 SOUTHWIRE PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Southwire Company, founded in 1950 by Roy Richards, is a leading manufacturer of wire and cable products, headquartered in Carrollton, Georgia. With over 8,500 employees across 38 facilities, Southwire serves construction, utility, and industrial markets, producing electrical cables, copper rods, and renewable energy solutions. Its 2023 revenue exceeded $8 billion. Southwires focus on innovation includes advanced conductor technologies and sustainable manufacturing, with initiatives like carbon-neutral goals by 2050. Acquisitions like Coleman Cable have expanded its portfolio. Competing with Prysmian and Nexans, Southwire emphasizes quality, safety, and community engagement through the Richards Family Foundation.
