value.Title: IKEA GROUP - UPDATES FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-KM23-RS4K-52KN-00000-00IKEA GROUP - UPDATES FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - UPDATES FROM THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA OPENS NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN TO ENHANCE CUSTOMIZED HOME SOLUTIONS

IKEA Group announced a new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia, set to open on the 22nd of April, 2026, with an 11 a.m. grand opening.The store is located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, and aims to improve access for Newnan residents. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, noted that the location reflects IKEA's ongoing expansion as part of its U.S. growth strategy.The Plan & Order Point offers planning help for items such as kitchens and bathrooms, with staff available to design and place orders. While customers cannot take products home the same day, delivery or free onsite pickup can be arranged.IKEA Group is expanding U.S. capabilities through investments in e-commerce, new IKEA Family benefits, improved shipping and delivery, Click & Collect, and TaskRabbit assembly assistance to support both in-store and online experiences.

+ IKEA'S AI TOOLKIT TRANSFORMS DESIGN, DELIVERY AND CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE

IKEA Group is pursuing an AI driven shift to an omni-channel model, with digital channels now about 30 percent of its business. During a visit to India on the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, explained how AI investments are reshaping customer experiences and operations.The strategy includes a Bengaluru digital hub and the Locus acquisition to boost last mile delivery, strengthening IKEA's presence in a fast growing app driven market.Internal tools such as AI based design software cut room planning time from hours to minutes, while the IKEA Kreativ platform helps customers visualize products at varied price points.The company frames these efforts as part of a sustainable growth plan with ethical AI guiding expansions in India and beyond.

+ VANTAA IKEA PILOT OFFERS PRE-BOOKED ASSISTANCE AND APP-GUIDED SHOPPING

IKEA Group is piloting an accessibility initiative at the Vantaa store in Finland, which began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping with NaviLens navigation to support customers with disabilities. The two main offerings are a 90-minute assisted-shopping session booked in advance with a trained coworker and the NaviLens app that provides audio guidance when shoppers scan codes around the store. Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland, says, "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs." The pilot is described as a learning platform to collect feedback and refine services for potential rollout elsewhere, reinforcing IKEA's commitment to inclusive journeys online and in-store with support from the Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise and international accessibility efforts.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) HARNESSES APP METRICS AND CREATOR PORTAL TO DRIVE TARGETED MARKETING

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is reshaping its marketing by merging technology, data analytics and influencer partnerships, according to Molly Battin on 6th May 2026. The retailer uses first-party data and regional weather signals to tailor recommendations in real time, while The Home Depot app drives higher spend—three to four times more than non-users—helping younger customers navigate stores through guided experiences. Store Mode assists in navigating the warehouse aisles, while the company’s creator portal expands partnerships with influencers who resonate with DIY communities. The Home Depot prioritizes creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values to customers. Campaigns tied to these efforts have supported new customer acquisition in niche categories, highlighting a broader shift in the CMO role toward technologically driven, financially accountable marketing and closer integration of digital tools with influencer networks.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) PRO ECOSYSTEM PROGRESS SUPPORTS ELEVATED VALUATIONS

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, several firms uplifted price targets into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism around Q4 results and the PRO pushGuggenheim and Wells Fargo highlighted progress in the PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings based on the companys strategic investments and long-term growth trajectorySome analysts remained cautious about the near-term, with RBC Capital and Bernstein assigning neutral views and lower targets. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets slightly amid concerns about remodeling demand and its impact on revenueHome Depot reaffirmed a 2026 total sales growth target of about 2.5% to 4.5% and rolled out PRO enhancements, including an expanded digital workspace for pros and a real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value remains US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions near 4.19% and some margin and P/E multiple adjustments as investors evaluate the PRO initiatives

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) REPORTS OPENING OF SAN MARCOS STORE BACKED BY $13 MILLION INVESTMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) is expanding into San Marcos with a $13 million store project confirmed on 28th April 2026. This will be the retailer’s first store in the college town, which has seen rapid growth in housing and retail. Plans filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) indicate a 135,918-square-foot footprint, including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction scheduled to start in September 2026 and to finish by August 2027. The new location sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and slated to open in October 2026. San Marcos, on the I-35 corridor and home to Texas State University, continues to attract development, aligning with Home Depot to broaden its presence. The company noted a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations nationwide

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PARTNERS WITH MADE IN TO LAUNCH EXCLUSIVE CAST IRON LINE

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) partners with Made In to launch an exclusive cast iron collection at Williams-Sonoma stores on 11th May 2026, with direct-to-consumer availability on Made In's site by June 1st. Williams-Sonoma will also stock Made In's American-made stainless steel collection, expanding its stainless cookware assortment. Made In, founded in 2017, counts more than 2,000 restaurant customers and saw revenue grow 52% from 2024 to 2025, with a large portion of sales in the fourth quarter. The cast iron line has been refined to be lighter while preserving performance, reflecting the brand's focus on usability. Long-running discussions with Williams-Sonoma culminated in a differentiated product launch. Made In also distributes through Crate & Barrel, Bloomingdale's and Ace Hardware, and plans a European fulfillment center by September as part of its international rollout.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) GOLDMAN SACHS BOOSTS RATING TO BUY WITH HIGHER PRICE TARGET

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a constructive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.The investment bank projects Williams-Sonoma to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates, while non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast at $13.00, down 33.82%. The update aligns with a broader trend of reassessing the home goods sector as consumer spending evolves.Investors will monitor how Williams-Sonoma translates this outlook into stronger revenue growth and margin discipline in the coming year.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) NAMES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO HEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined in 2003 as a Pottery Barn district manager and rose through the Rejuvenation banner to become senior vice president in 2020.In her new role, she will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives to drive Rejuvenation within the overall brand portfolio.CEO Laura Alber noted that Taylor's leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to reach billion-dollar potential, reflecting her track record and design-oriented approach.Rejuvenation has expanded with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has partnered on a Roll & Hill lighting collection. Williams-Sonoma has named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The firm projects 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE REFRAMES BRAND WITH LUXE LINE TO ENTER HIGHER PRICE POINTS

Ashley is moving into upscale with Luxe, which was unveiled May 1, 2026. The initiative represents a notable shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores and has historically centered its assortments on value-driven midrange products.Luxe is a full-line offering including living, dining, and bedroom furniture. It will be available in about 70% of Ashley's stores, via enterprise and licensee partners as well as online. Price points position Luxe above budget lines but below luxury brands, with sofas typically $1,000–$1,200 and many sectionals from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, described Luxe as a way to reach new customers and differentiate the brand in a crowded market.The line leverages Ashley's existing supply chain, mixing domestic manufacturing with imports to preserve pricing and quality. Stores will feature dedicated Luxe areas with custom signage, and the Las Vegas flagship will showcase the line as a focal point. A campaign with Hailee Steinfeld supports the launch, and Todd Wanek calls Luxe an evolution that remains within reach.

+ SPANAWAY HUB PURCHASE SIGNALS AMAZON'S GROWING E-COMMERCE LOGISTICS PUSH

Amazon has bought Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—about 1.1 million square feet along with nearby parcels—for more than $200 million, as reported on the 27th of April 2026.The move strengthens Amazon's logistics network in the Pacific Northwest, aiding faster deliveries as e-commerce grows.Ashley Furniture is divesting the property as part of a strategic realignment, leaving questions about its future distribution plans.The sale spotlights the brisk commercial real estate activity in the Pacific Northwest and Amazon's continued push into large-scale warehousing.

+ FRONTLINEIQ DEBUTS INDUSTRY-LEADING AI COACHING FOR IN-PERSON SALES AT HIGH POINT

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the AI sales coaching platform for in-person teams, announced the Ashley AI Advantage, developed with Ashley Furniture Industries, at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026.The program introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time conversation coaching for frontline associates, enabling practice with real customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven selling methods.The Ashley AI Advantage is free for Ashley Wholesale customers and licensees, with scalable options. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, called it transformative, while Ashley's John Mask said it offers a new level of showroom-floor confidence and consistency.As a turnkey entry point pre-configured for home furnishings, the platform can be customized for retailers' training processes. More information is available at www.frontlineiq.ai/ashley.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) LAUNCHES MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD FOR CONTRACTORS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card with Synchrony for professional contractors, usable anywhere American Express is accepted, as announced on the 6th of May 2026. Built to support a contractor-focused strategy, the card extends payment options beyond Lowe's stores to deepen relationships with pro buyers. The company projects 2029 revenue of $100.6 billion and earnings of $8.1 billion, with revenue growth of 5.3% annually. Near term, analysts expect limited earnings impact as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides a 2026 comp outlook of flat to low single digits

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORES ACROSS GROWTH MARKETS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Expands Footprint With New Stores Across Growth MarketsLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space and a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, with more than 100 associates on staff. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will showcase redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms that blend style, affordability and innovation. New showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Other openings are planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. The expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet growing demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. District Manager Christian Redman highlighted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is positioned to deliver value, convenience and service to the community. Investors will assess how these locations contribute to sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) OPENS 94,000-SQ.-FT PORT ST. LUCIE STORE AS PART OF GROWTH PLAN

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) unveils expansion plans including a Port St. Lucie location in Florida sized at about 94,000 square feet with a 30,000-square-foot garden center and more than 100 staff, slated to open in early June 2026. The company also plans four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky to begin operations later this year.The new stores will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for quick paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup to enhance convenience for customers.Lowe's operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, and handles roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales exceeded $83 billion. The MyLowe's Rewards program provides points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, reinforcing customer loyalty

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXTENDS HEMP-DERIVED THC BEVERAGE PILOT TO THREE ADDITIONAL STATES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) expands its hemp-derived THC beverage pilot beyond Minnesota to Florida, Illinois and Texas, effective May 11, 2026, subject to local regulations. The Minnesota launch laid the groundwork for a broader geographic test of product availability and guest interest.Brands Cann, Wynk, Trail Magic, Stigma, Gigli and Daizy’s will be available across stores in the three new states, complementing the existing Minnesota rollout.A Target spokesperson states the retailer is "always exploring new ways to meet our guests’ evolving preferences, grounded in our merchandising authority and focus on thoughtfully curating a relevant assortment." This expansion aims to measure consumer response and supply-chain readiness amid a regulatory landscape that could shift with federal action on hemp-derived products, a market currently estimated at $28 billion

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) REPLACES AFFILIATE MODEL WITH DUAL CREATOR INITIATIVES TO DRIVE DIGITAL ENGAGEMENT

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is restructuring its social-marketing framework by moving to two creator programs, Club Target and Target Ambassadors, effective 10th May 2026, replacing the previous affiliate program. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) notes that the approach aligns rewards more closely with engagement and sales, with Ambassadors focusing on high-reach voices and Club Target helping everyday shoppers participate as creators. Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) also highlights the broader shift of shopping into the digital environment, where peers like Walmart and Amazon are actively investing

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) SETS PATH TO RECOVERY WITH PARKE COLLABORATION, RBC CAPITAL RAISES TARGET TO $132

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Posts early signs of progress in its turnaround as RBC Capital reports a higher price target of $132 from $130 and an Outperform rating ahead of the Q1 results on 9th May 2026On April 21, Target rolled out a limited-time collaboration with Parke, a premium brand for Gen Z shoppers, through its community-driven approach that aims to reflect consumer preferences in product developmentTarget remains a general merchandise retailer delivering everyday essentials and differentiated products via stores and digital channels with a focus on value for its guestsRBC notes that while Target has momentum, some AI stocks offer greater upside potential with lower risk, underscoring a broader investment landscape for investors

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: IKEA GROUP - THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJT-MCJ3-RRXW-T17Y-00000-00IKEA GROUP - THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA OPENS NEW PLAN & ORDER POINT IN NEWNAN TO ENHANCE CUSTOMIZED HOME SOLUTIONS

IKEA Group announced a new Plan & Order Point with pick-up in Newnan, Georgia, set to open on the 22nd of April, 2026, with an 11 a.m. grand opening.The store is located in Ashley Park at 378 Newnan Crossing Bypass, Suite 378, and aims to improve access for Newnan residents. Brandon Lomax, IKEA Atlanta Market Manager, noted that the location reflects IKEA's ongoing expansion as part of its U.S. growth strategy.The Plan & Order Point offers planning help for items such as kitchens and bathrooms, with staff available to design and place orders. While customers cannot take products home the same day, delivery or free onsite pickup can be arranged.IKEA Group is expanding U.S. capabilities through investments in e-commerce, new IKEA Family benefits, improved shipping and delivery, Click & Collect, and TaskRabbit assembly assistance to support both in-store and online experiences.

+ IKEA'S AI TOOLKIT TRANSFORMS DESIGN, DELIVERY AND CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE

IKEA Group is pursuing an AI driven shift to an omni-channel model, with digital channels now about 30 percent of its business. During a visit to India on the 4th of May 2026, Parag Parekh, Ingka Group's Global Chief Digital Officer, explained how AI investments are reshaping customer experiences and operations.The strategy includes a Bengaluru digital hub and the Locus acquisition to boost last mile delivery, strengthening IKEA's presence in a fast growing app driven market.Internal tools such as AI based design software cut room planning time from hours to minutes, while the IKEA Kreativ platform helps customers visualize products at varied price points.The company frames these efforts as part of a sustainable growth plan with ethical AI guiding expansions in India and beyond.

+ VANTAA IKEA PILOT OFFERS PRE-BOOKED ASSISTANCE AND APP-GUIDED SHOPPING

IKEA Group is piloting an accessibility initiative at the Vantaa store in Finland, which began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping with NaviLens navigation to support customers with disabilities. The two main offerings are a 90-minute assisted-shopping session booked in advance with a trained coworker and the NaviLens app that provides audio guidance when shoppers scan codes around the store. Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland, says, "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs." The pilot is described as a learning platform to collect feedback and refine services for potential rollout elsewhere, reinforcing IKEA's commitment to inclusive journeys online and in-store with support from the Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise and international accessibility efforts.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) HARNESSES APP METRICS AND CREATOR PORTAL TO DRIVE TARGETED MARKETING

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is reshaping its marketing by merging technology, data analytics and influencer partnerships, according to Molly Battin on 6th May 2026. The retailer uses first-party data and regional weather signals to tailor recommendations in real time, while The Home Depot app drives higher spend—three to four times more than non-users—helping younger customers navigate stores through guided experiences. Store Mode assists in navigating the warehouse aisles, while the company’s creator portal expands partnerships with influencers who resonate with DIY communities. The Home Depot prioritizes creators who authentically reflect the brand and can communicate its values to customers. Campaigns tied to these efforts have supported new customer acquisition in niche categories, highlighting a broader shift in the CMO role toward technologically driven, financially accountable marketing and closer integration of digital tools with influencer networks.

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) PRO ECOSYSTEM PROGRESS SUPPORTS ELEVATED VALUATIONS

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) On the 5th of May 2026, several firms uplifted price targets into the high US$300s to mid US$400s, with UBS at US$450 and Jefferies at US$454, reflecting optimism around Q4 results and the PRO pushGuggenheim and Wells Fargo highlighted progress in the PRO ecosystem, while Goldman Sachs and Telsey Advisory maintained positive ratings based on the companys strategic investments and long-term growth trajectorySome analysts remained cautious about the near-term, with RBC Capital and Bernstein assigning neutral views and lower targets. Piper Sandler and Goldman Sachs trimmed targets slightly amid concerns about remodeling demand and its impact on revenueHome Depot reaffirmed a 2026 total sales growth target of about 2.5% to 4.5% and rolled out PRO enhancements, including an expanded digital workspace for pros and a real-time delivery tracker for large materials. The fair value remains US$408.21 per share, with revenue growth assumptions near 4.19% and some margin and P/E multiple adjustments as investors evaluate the PRO initiatives

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) REPORTS OPENING OF SAN MARCOS STORE BACKED BY $13 MILLION INVESTMENT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) is expanding into San Marcos with a $13 million store project confirmed on 28th April 2026. This will be the retailer’s first store in the college town, which has seen rapid growth in housing and retail. Plans filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) indicate a 135,918-square-foot footprint, including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction scheduled to start in September 2026 and to finish by August 2027. The new location sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and slated to open in October 2026. San Marcos, on the I-35 corridor and home to Texas State University, continues to attract development, aligning with Home Depot to broaden its presence. The company noted a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations nationwide

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) NAMES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO HEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined in 2003 as a Pottery Barn district manager and rose through the Rejuvenation banner to become senior vice president in 2020.In her new role, she will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives to drive Rejuvenation within the overall brand portfolio.CEO Laura Alber noted that Taylor's leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to reach billion-dollar potential, reflecting her track record and design-oriented approach.Rejuvenation has expanded with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has partnered on a Roll & Hill lighting collection. Williams-Sonoma has named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The firm projects 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) EVERCORE SETS $200 TARGET AND KEEPS HOLD RATING

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) maintains a Hold rating as Evercore raises its price target to $200 from $195 in a 14th April 2026 report. Analyst Oliver Wintermantel, who leads Williams-Sonoma coverage at Evercore, has a 66.7% success rate and a 23.9% average return over the past year, according to TipRanks. The target adjustment reflects Evercore’s view of market dynamics and Williams-Sonoma’s operational strategies within a competitive home-goods landscape. While the rating remains Hold, the new target suggests investors should monitor seasonal trends, inventory management and supply-chain efficiency as Williams-Sonoma pursues its projected performance.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) GOLDMAN SACHS BOOSTS RATING TO BUY WITH HIGHER PRICE TARGET

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a constructive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.The investment bank projects Williams-Sonoma to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates, while non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast at $13.00, down 33.82%. The update aligns with a broader trend of reassessing the home goods sector as consumer spending evolves.Investors will monitor how Williams-Sonoma translates this outlook into stronger revenue growth and margin discipline in the coming year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE REFRAMES BRAND WITH LUXE LINE TO ENTER HIGHER PRICE POINTS

Ashley is moving into upscale with Luxe, which was unveiled May 1, 2026. The initiative represents a notable shift for the nation's largest furniture retailer, which operates more than 1,100 stores and has historically centered its assortments on value-driven midrange products.Luxe is a full-line offering including living, dining, and bedroom furniture. It will be available in about 70% of Ashley's stores, via enterprise and licensee partners as well as online. Price points position Luxe above budget lines but below luxury brands, with sofas typically $1,000–$1,200 and many sectionals from $2,900 to $6,000.Lisa Fanaro, executive vice president of brand strategy and experience, described Luxe as a way to reach new customers and differentiate the brand in a crowded market.The line leverages Ashley's existing supply chain, mixing domestic manufacturing with imports to preserve pricing and quality. Stores will feature dedicated Luxe areas with custom signage, and the Las Vegas flagship will showcase the line as a focal point. A campaign with Hailee Steinfeld supports the launch, and Todd Wanek calls Luxe an evolution that remains within reach.

+ SPANAWAY HUB PURCHASE SIGNALS AMAZON'S GROWING E-COMMERCE LOGISTICS PUSH

Amazon has bought Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—about 1.1 million square feet along with nearby parcels—for more than $200 million, as reported on the 27th of April 2026.The move strengthens Amazon's logistics network in the Pacific Northwest, aiding faster deliveries as e-commerce grows.Ashley Furniture is divesting the property as part of a strategic realignment, leaving questions about its future distribution plans.The sale spotlights the brisk commercial real estate activity in the Pacific Northwest and Amazon's continued push into large-scale warehousing.

+ FRONTLINEIQ DEBUTS INDUSTRY-LEADING AI COACHING FOR IN-PERSON SALES AT HIGH POINT

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the AI sales coaching platform for in-person teams, announced the Ashley AI Advantage, developed with Ashley Furniture Industries, at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026.The program introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time conversation coaching for frontline associates, enabling practice with real customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven selling methods.The Ashley AI Advantage is free for Ashley Wholesale customers and licensees, with scalable options. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, called it transformative, while Ashley's John Mask said it offers a new level of showroom-floor confidence and consistency.As a turnkey entry point pre-configured for home furnishings, the platform can be customized for retailers' training processes. More information is available at www.frontlineiq.ai/ashley.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) LAUNCHES MYLOWE'S PRO REWARDS AMERICAN EXPRESS CARD FOR CONTRACTORS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card with Synchrony for professional contractors, usable anywhere American Express is accepted, as announced on the 6th of May 2026. Built to support a contractor-focused strategy, the card extends payment options beyond Lowe's stores to deepen relationships with pro buyers. The company projects 2029 revenue of $100.6 billion and earnings of $8.1 billion, with revenue growth of 5.3% annually. Near term, analysts expect limited earnings impact as Lowe's reports elevated debt from the FBM acquisition and guides a 2026 comp outlook of flat to low single digits

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORES ACROSS GROWTH MARKETS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Expands Footprint With New Stores Across Growth MarketsLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space and a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, with more than 100 associates on staff. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will showcase redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms that blend style, affordability and innovation. New showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Other openings are planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. The expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet growing demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. District Manager Christian Redman highlighted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is positioned to deliver value, convenience and service to the community. Investors will assess how these locations contribute to sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) OPENS 94,000-SQ.-FT PORT ST. LUCIE STORE AS PART OF GROWTH PLAN

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) unveils expansion plans including a Port St. Lucie location in Florida sized at about 94,000 square feet with a 30,000-square-foot garden center and more than 100 staff, slated to open in early June 2026. The company also plans four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky to begin operations later this year.The new stores will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for quick paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup to enhance convenience for customers.Lowe's operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, and handles roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales exceeded $83 billion. The MyLowe's Rewards program provides points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, reinforcing customer loyalty

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) BETS ON SPEED WITH NATIONWIDE NEXT-DAY DELIVERY EXPANSION AFTER 2025 SALES DECLINE

As Of The 28th Of April 2026, Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Expands Next-Day Delivery To 60% Of The U.S. Population, Reaching Over 50 Metro Areas, With Many Deliveries FreeThe Move Emphasizes Speed In Online Shopping And Supports Target's Efforts To Rebuild Shopper TrustEligible Items Rose To 85% Of All In-Store Selections, With Orders Placed By 6 P.M. Local Time Arriving The Next Day; Standard Delivery Is $5.99, And Orders Over $35 Qualify For Free DeliveryThe Initiative Is Part Of The New Chapter $5 Billion Strategy Led By Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke, Following A 1.7% Net Sales Decline In 2025, And Includes Store Refreshes And New Collaborations

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) NON-MERCHANDISE REVENUE SURGES 25% IN QUARTER

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) posts fiscal 2025 results for the period ending 31st January 2026, with stock up 29% since the start of 2026. E-commerce sales reach 20.6% of total merchandise sales, and the fourth quarter e-commerce share rose to 23.7%. Non-merchandise sales rose 25% in the quarter, with membership revenue more than doubling year over year. Target operates as a general merchandise retailer with stores and digital platforms, delivering a mix of everyday essentials and differentiated merchandise to customers. Analysts note that opportunities in AI and other sectors may offer upside as market dynamics evolve

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) PARTNERS WITH LOCAL NONPROFITS TO REVITALIZE COMMUNITY SPACES ACROSS 13 CITIES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) expands its Bullseye Builds with Community Program, a design-driven effort to revitalize community spaces in 13 U.S. cities with a $1 million investment. The initiative will begin on 21st May 2026 in Denver and Las VegasLaunched in 2024, the program has completed 25 projects to date and supported more than 250,000 community members with help from Target volunteers. In Denver, Trevista at Horace Mann Elementary School will upgrade the student wellness room and create outdoor learning spaces; In Las Vegas, The Just One Project will address food insecurity and improve shopping areas and pantry facilities. Each project will involve more than 100 volunteers from TargetOther cities include Austin, Birmingham, Columbus, Jersey City, Logan Township, Orlando, Washington, D.C., Minneapolis-St. Paul, Phoenix, St. Louis, and Seattle. "At Target, we believe investing in the places we call home builds strong communities and strong partnerships," said Kiera Fernandez, Executive Vice President and Chief Community and Stakeholder Engagement Officer. The company also notes that team members contributed 1 million volunteer hours in the U.S. in 2025

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: U.S. solar energy firm plans $750 million power grid expansion across four African countries
value.Overview: Business Insider Africa, RENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS, Utilities news, US news & AFRICA NEWS, 447words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ1-FC83-SGS6-12D1-00000-00U.S. solar energy firm plans $750 million power grid expansion across four African countries2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS, Utilities news, US news & AFRICA NEWSU.S. solar energy firm plans $750 million power grid expansion across four African countriesAdekunle Agbetiloye

Link to Image

Renewvia Energy Corp. is expanding its solar-powered mini-grid operations into four African countries as renewable energy providers increasingly move to address the continent's widening electricity deficit.

Renewvia Energy Corp. is expanding its solar-powered mini-grid operations into Uganda, Rwanda, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of Congo.

The company plans an investment of about $750 million to provide roughly 2.1 million electricity connections across these countries.

Sub-Saharan Africa faces significant energy poverty, with nearly 600 million people lacking access to electricity.

Renewvia currently operates 24 mini-grids in Kenya and Nigeria and is seeking $45 million to expand energy access in major refugee settlements like Kakuma and Dadaab.

Renewvia Energy Corp. is expanding its solar-powered mini-grid operations into four African countries as renewable energy providers increasingly move to address the continent's widening electricity deficit.

The Atlanta-based company plans to expand into Uganda, Rwanda, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of Congo, a move expected to require about $750 million in investment for roughly 2.1 million electricity connections, according to Renewvia Solar Africa CEO Trey Jarrard.

Sub-Saharan Africa remains the global epicentre of energy poverty, with nearly 600 million people lacking access to electricity, accounting for more than 80% of the world's unelectrified population.

Efforts to close the gap have intensified under the World Bank and African Development Bank-backed Mission 300 initiative, which aims to connect 300 million Africans to electricity by 2030, Bloomberg reported.

Renewvia expands African footprint

Renewvia already operates 24 commercial mini-grids across Kenya and Nigeria, ranging from 100 kilowatts to 2.5 megawatts. The systems supply electricity to rural communities and commercial clients, including Shell, UBA Bank, and UNHCR.

The company is also seeking $45 million in concessional financing to expand a metro-grid in Kakuma and develop a renewable energy plant in Dadaab, two of the world's largest refugee settlements.

Focus on refugee settlements

According to Jarrard, the financing would allow Renewvia to keep electricity tariffs affordable through longer-term, lower-interest loans. The project could increase electricity access in Kakuma and Dadaab fivefold, potentially reaching more than 550,000 people.

Renewvia has also established local entities in Congo, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ethiopia to support early-stage development efforts. One of its proposed projects includes a mini-grid in Baraka, a town on the shores of Lake Tanganyika with a population of about 270,000 people.

value.Title: Cox Enterprises names new executives

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJY-43D3-RTF4-204G-00000-00Cox Enterprises names new executives2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisCox Enterprises names new executivesLibby Allnatt

Cox Enterprises is shuffling its C-suite.

Dallas Clement will take on the role of chief operating officer, according to a May 13 announcement. Currently the president and chief financial officer, Clement has been at Cox for 35 years, holding executive roles across all divisions of the company.

Awarding him the COO title formalizes responsibilities Clement had already been handling: oversight of Cox Enterprises' growth operations, strategy and investments, and corporate development organizations, as well as Socium Ventures, the company's venture capital fund.

Clement will remain president, too, but hand off his CFO duties. He will report to Alex Taylor, who has been CEO and chair of Cox Enterprises since 2018.

"As we continue shaping what's next for Cox Enterprises, we will be highly intentional in how we deploy capital to invest in our growth and diversification strategies," Taylor said in a statement. "Dallas is a longtime Cox leader, and there are few people that I trust more than him. I have complete confidence in his ability to continue driving the next phase of growth for Cox and the expansion of our portfolio of companies."

Perley McBride will become chief financial and administrative officer, with responsibility for the company's finance, technology, corporate services and sustainability organizations.

McBride has been the executive vice president and CFO of Cox Communications since 2018. Prior to joining Cox Communications, he held CFO roles at Frontier Communications, Cable & Wireless PLC, Cricket Communications and Leap Wireless. He also served as EVP of finance for The Weather Channel.

Cox Communications and Charter Communications Inc. are planning to combine their businesses in a deal that was approved by the Federal Communications Commission earlier this year. The proposed transaction values Cox Communications at an enterprise value of approximately $34.5 billion.

Clement and McBride will officially transition into their new roles this summer.

Taylor said the leadership changes support a vision for "an evolved Cox Enterprises."

"Continuing to diversify our family of businesses and create new opportunities is one of the most important things we will be focused on going forward," he said in a statement.

With about 50,000 employees, Cox Enterprises is Atlanta's largest private company, according to research from Atlanta Business Chronicle. It owns, operates and invests in businesses across multiple industries, including automotive, broadband and mobile, greenhouse agriculture and renewable energy.

Cox Enterprises is the parent company of The Atlanta Journal-Constitution, which just announced a leadership change of its own. Andrew Morse is stepping down as president and publisher after three years in the role, with Paul Curran, a veteran of Cox Media Group and Cox Communications, taking on the role.

Did you find this article useful? Why not subscribe to Atlanta Business Chronicle for more articles?

value.Title: Evan Davitashvili Appointed CEO of Italiana Petro

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JNW-C253-SFYP-9441-00000-00Evan Davitashvili Appointed CEO of Italiana Petro2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisEvan Davitashvili Appointed CEO of Italiana Petro

Key Highlights:

* Levan Davitashvili appointed CEO, Managing Director of Italiana Petroli.

* API Holding acquires 99.82% of Italiana Petroli's capital.

* Davitashvili to lead IP's operations in Italy and integrate with SOCAR Group.

Original Press Release:

Baku/Rome,May 13 -- Italiana Petroli SpA issued the following news release:

Following the completion of the acquisition of 99.82% of the share capital of Italiana Petroli (IP) from API Holding, SOCAR announces the appointment of Levan Davitashvili as CEO and Managing Director of IP.

In this role, Davitashvili will lead IP's operations in Italy, with the aim of ensuring operational continuity, preserving and further strengthening its position within the national energy market, and overseeing the company's integration into the broader SOCAR Group.

This appointment reflects SOCAR's commitment to Italy through dedicated leadership-leadership capable of combining the Group's international industrial strength and strategic vision with a deep understanding of the local market and institutional landscape.

Davitashvili, who is fluent in Italian, brings with him over 25 years of experience in executive positions across both the public and private sectors. From 2021 to 2025, he served as First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Economy and Sustainable Development of Georgia, leading the national economic strategy and driving the implementation of major infrastructure initiatives. He also spearheaded the country's involvement in the Green Energy Corridor initiative, which connects the South Caucasus to European Union member states. Since November 2025, he has served as an Advisor to the President of SOCAR.

SOCAR

SOCAR, a multinational energy company headquartered in Azerbaijan, specializes in the extraction, processing, and distribution of energy resources. As the largest energy company in the South Caucasus region, the firm boasts a significant global presence, underscoring its prominence across various international markets.

value.Title: May 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Expand Scholarships to Grow Skilled Trades

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G2BY-00000-00May 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Expand Scholarships to Grow Skilled Trades2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:02ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: Southwire and SKILLED Nation Expand Scholarships to Grow Skilled Trades

On 07th May 2026, Southwire and SKILLED Nation announced an expansion of their joint scholarship program tied to the 12 for Life initiative, strengthening support for high school graduates pursuing skilled trades. The collaboration has already disbursed more than $200,000 to students across Georgia and Alabama.

The Southwire 12 for Life SKILLED Scholarship, launched two years ago, supports community college, technical, or vocational education. The latest round will add $100,000 in scholarships this year, underscoring the company's commitment to local communities.

Among the first recipients is Magnum Cannon, a graduate of the 12 for Life program studying Industrial Systems Technology and HVAC at Northwest Shoals Community College. He said, "Receiving this scholarship means a lot to me." Another recipient, Robert Anthony, noted that the scholarship relieved financial burdens and allowed him to focus on his studies at West Georgia Technical College. Burt Fealing, Executive Vice President at Southwire, stated, "These scholarships open doors to meaningful careers and lasting achievement." The partners say the effort strengthens the local economy by promoting skilled labor and addressing workforce needs.

INDEX

SECTION 1 SOUTHWIRE PROFILE

SECTION 2 SOUTHWIRE TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 3 SOUTHWIRE RANKING

SECTION 1 SOUTHWIRE PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Southwire Company, founded in 1950 by Roy Richards, is a leading manufacturer of wire and cable products, headquartered in Carrollton, Georgia. With over 8,500 employees across 38 facilities, Southwire serves construction, utility, and industrial markets, producing electrical cables, copper rods, and renewable energy solutions. Its 2023 revenue exceeded $8 billion. Southwires focus on innovation includes advanced conductor technologies and sustainable manufacturing, with initiatives like carbon-neutral goals by 2050. Acquisitions like Coleman Cable have expanded its portfolio. Competing with Prysmian and Nexans, Southwire emphasizes quality, safety, and community engagement through the Richards Family Foundation.

value.Title: Research and Markets Publishes Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 2026

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-4723-RSJV-W37P-00000-00Research and Markets Publishes Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 20262026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisResearch and Markets Publishes Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 2026

The "Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Market Report 2026" has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offering.

According to a release, the neodymium iron boron (NdFeB) permanent magnets market has experienced robust growth recently, with a projected expansion from $26.45 billion in 2025 to $28.34 billion by 2026, registering a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.1 percent. This growth trajectory can be linked to consumer electronics' miniaturization, increased demand for high-efficiency industrial motors, expansion of renewable energy sources like wind turbines, and a strong dependence on rare earth supply chains.

Forecasts indicate the NdFeB permanent magnets market will continue its upward climb, reaching $37.67 billion in 2030 with a CAGR of 7.4 percent. Factors propelling this growth include rising demand for electric vehicle traction motors, greater investment in advanced manufacturing methods, the need for lightweight magnetic components, expanding applications in medical device miniaturization, and a push towards recycling and sustainable rare earth sourcing. Emerging trends include a surge in demand for high-performance miniaturized magnets, development of high-temperature grade NdFeB magnets, adoption of corrosion-resistant coatings, and the utilization of 3D-printed magnet geometries.

The burgeoning demand for electric vehicles (EVs) is a significant driver for the NdFeB magnets market. EVs, reliant on high-performance permanent magnets like NdFeB for efficient motors, are on the rise due to stricter emission norms and a global shift towards clean mobility solutions. According to Kelley Blue Book, 2023 saw a record 1.2 million U.S. car buyers opting for EVs, representing 7.6 percent of the market, up from 5.9 percent in 2022.

To bolster supply chain resilience amid rising demand, companies in NdFeB magnets are expanding manufacturing capacities outside China. Initiatives aim to enhance production through new facilities and equipment upgrades. For instance, JS Link announced in 2025 a new production facility in Georgia, USA, expected to produce three kilotons of NdFeB magnets annually, with operations set for 2027, boosting non-Chinese supply capacity.

In May 2023, Neo Performance Materials acquired SG Technologies Group for $13.4 million, enhancing its NdFeB manufacturing capabilities in Europe. This move aims to support demand from sectors like electric vehicles, electronics, and clean energy. Key players in the market include TDK Corp., Daido Steel, Neo Performance Materials, and more.

NdFeB permanent magnets are essential for their strong magnetic performance in compact applications. They are available in forms including bonded, sintered, and 3D-printed magnets, suited to various industry needs from automotive to medical devices. In essence, the NdFeB permanent magnets market is poised for continued growth, driven by technological advancements and increasing demand from electric mobility and sustainable energy sectors.

Key Technologies & Future Trends

-High-Performance Miniaturized Magnet Demand

-Expansion Of High-Temperature Grade Ndfeb Magnets

-Rising Adoption Of Corrosion-Resistant Coatings

-Growth In Customized Magnetic Assemblies

-Increasing Use Of 3D-Printed Magnet Geometries

-Electric Mobility & Transportation Electrification

-Sustainability, Climate Tech & Circular Economy

-Autonomous Systems, Robotics & Smart Mobility

-Industry 4.0 & Intelligent Manufacturing

-Internet Of Things (Iot), Smart Infrastructure & Connected Ecosystems

The "Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Market Report 2026" has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offering.

According to a release, the neodymium iron boron (NdFeB) permanent magnets market has experienced robust growth recently, with a projected expansion from $26.45 billion in 2025 to $28.34 billion by 2026, registering a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.1 percent. This growth trajectory can be linked to consumer electronics' miniaturization, increased demand for high-efficiency industrial motors, expansion of renewable energy sources like wind turbines, and a strong dependence on rare earth supply chains.

Forecasts indicate the NdFeB permanent magnets market will continue its upward climb, reaching $37.67 billion in 2030 with a CAGR of 7.4 percent. Factors propelling this growth include rising demand for electric vehicle traction motors, greater investment in advanced manufacturing methods, the need for lightweight magnetic components, expanding applications in medical device miniaturization, and a push towards recycling and sustainable rare earth sourcing. Emerging trends include a surge in demand for high-performance miniaturized magnets, development of high-temperature grade NdFeB magnets, adoption of corrosion-resistant coatings, and the utilization of 3D-printed magnet geometries.

The burgeoning demand for electric vehicles (EVs) is a significant driver for the NdFeB magnets market. EVs, reliant on high-performance permanent magnets like NdFeB for efficient motors, are on the rise due to stricter emission norms and a global shift towards clean mobility solutions. According to Kelley Blue Book, 2023 saw a record 1.2 million U.S. car buyers opting for EVs, representing 7.6 percent of the market, up from 5.9 percent in 2022.

To bolster supply chain resilience amid rising demand, companies in NdFeB magnets are expanding manufacturing capacities outside China. Initiatives aim to enhance production through new facilities and equipment upgrades. For instance, JS Link announced in 2025 a new production facility in Georgia, USA, expected to produce three kilotons of NdFeB magnets annually, with operations set for 2027, boosting non-Chinese supply capacity.

In May 2023, Neo Performance Materials acquired SG Technologies Group for $13.4 million, enhancing its NdFeB manufacturing capabilities in Europe. This move aims to support demand from sectors like electric vehicles, electronics, and clean energy. Key players in the market include TDK Corp., Daido Steel, Neo Performance Materials, and more.

NdFeB permanent magnets are essential for their strong magnetic performance in compact applications. They are available in forms including bonded, sintered, and 3D-printed magnets, suited to various industry needs from automotive to medical devices. In essence, the NdFeB permanent magnets market is poised for continued growth, driven by technological advancements and increasing demand from electric mobility and sustainable energy sectors.

value.Title: Research and Markets Highlights Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 2026 - $37.67 Billion Market Opportunities

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGX-6W93-S8B6-G41G-00000-00Research and Markets Highlights Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 2026 - $37.67 Billion Market Opportunities2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisResearch and Markets Highlights Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 2026 - $37.67 Billion Market Opportunities

The "Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Market Report 2026" has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offering.

According to a release, the neodymium iron boron (NdFeB) permanent magnets market has experienced robust growth recently, with a projected expansion from $26.45 billion in 2025 to $28.34 billion by 2026, registering a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.1 percent. This growth trajectory can be linked to consumer electronics' miniaturization, increased demand for high-efficiency industrial motors, expansion of renewable energy sources like wind turbines, and a strong dependence on rare earth supply chains.

Forecasts indicate the NdFeB permanent magnets market will continue its upward climb, reaching $37.67 billion in 2030 with a CAGR of 7.4 percent. Factors propelling this growth include rising demand for electric vehicle traction motors, greater investment in advanced manufacturing methods, the need for lightweight magnetic components, expanding applications in medical device miniaturization, and a push towards recycling and sustainable rare earth sourcing. Emerging trends include a surge in demand for high-performance miniaturized magnets, development of high-temperature grade NdFeB magnets, adoption of corrosion-resistant coatings, and the utilization of 3D-printed magnet geometries.

The burgeoning demand for electric vehicles (EVs) is a significant driver for the NdFeB magnets market. EVs, reliant on high-performance permanent magnets like NdFeB for efficient motors, are on the rise due to stricter emission norms and a global shift towards clean mobility solutions. According to Kelley Blue Book, 2023 saw a record 1.2 million U.S. car buyers opting for EVs, representing 7.6 percent of the market, up from 5.9 percent in 2022.

To bolster supply chain resilience amid rising demand, companies in NdFeB magnets are expanding manufacturing capacities outside China. Initiatives aim to enhance production through new facilities and equipment upgrades. For instance, JS Link announced in 2025 a new production facility in Georgia, USA, expected to produce three kilotons of NdFeB magnets annually, with operations set for 2027, boosting non-Chinese supply capacity.

In May 2023, Neo Performance Materials acquired SG Technologies Group for $13.4 million, enhancing its NdFeB manufacturing capabilities in Europe. This move aims to support demand from sectors like electric vehicles, electronics, and clean energy. Key players in the market include TDK Corp., Daido Steel, Neo Performance Materials, and more.

NdFeB permanent magnets are essential for their strong magnetic performance in compact applications. They are available in forms including bonded, sintered, and 3D-printed magnets, suited to various industry needs from automotive to medical devices. In essence, the NdFeB permanent magnets market is poised for continued growth, driven by technological advancements and increasing demand from electric mobility and sustainable energy sectors.

value.Title: May 01, 2026: TURNER INDUSTRIES GROUP : ANALYZING LATEST PEER GROUP NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGN-S093-S3VB-54KF-00000-00May 01, 2026: TURNER INDUSTRIES GROUP: ANALYZING LATEST PEER GROUP NEWS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 01, 2026: TURNER INDUSTRIES GROUP: ANALYZING LATEST PEER GROUP NEWS

Turner Industries Group and its peer set recorded a series of notable developments across acquisitions, leadership updates, infrastructure delivery, environmental programs, and AI-related contract activity during 2025 and 2026. For Turner Industries Group, one of the latest reported developments came through Turner Supply, which announced the acquisition of Progressive Supply, a Woodstock, Georgia-based provider of maintenance, repair, and operations solutions. The transaction closed on January 31, 2026, and marked Turner Supply's entry into the Atlanta and North Georgia markets through its ninth location. Progressive Supply had built its position serving customers in construction, manufacturing, welding, metal fabrication, and utilities, which aligned with Turner Supply's existing market base. Chip Schramm, CEO and President of Turner Supply, said the acquired company's "core values and culture of high touch customer service are a strong match," while Progressive Supply owner Rick Klein said continuity for customers was a priority and that Turner would add capabilities and resources without changing the existing service approach.

Another Turner-related development centered on executive recognition. Roland Toups, executive chairman of Turner Industries, received the Lifetime Achievement Award at the inaugural Louisiana Energy Awards presented by 10/12 Industry Report in Baton Rouge on June 13, 2025. The recognition highlighted Toups' long tenure and his role in the company's growth after joining Turner Industries in 1968 as an engineer. Over the course of his career, he helped guide the company from a local construction firm into a multibillion-dollar industrial contractor with more than 20,000 employees across the United States. The award also reflected his public service work, including leadership positions with the Louisiana State Board of Regents, the Greater Baton Rouge Chamber of Commerce, and the Louisiana Chemical Industry Alliance. The Louisiana Energy Awards recognized contributions across oil and gas, carbon capture, hydrogen, and renewable energy, with categories spanning Lifetime Achievement, Energy Executive of the Year, and Energy Company of the Year.

Among peers, Bechtel's recent news flow included pressure on project delivery, leadership transition, and higher-education investment linked to construction talent development. In Serbia, President Aleksandar Vu i said on April 21, 2026, that he had met representatives of the Bechtel-Enka consortium to discuss progress on the Moravian Corridor highway. He emphasized adherence to deadlines, particularly on the a ak-Kraljevo section, which he described as highly important for western and southwestern Serbia. The road project has been characterized as one of the country's most complex infrastructure efforts in recent history, and the Serbian government has tied its timely completion to broader regional connectivity, domestic commerce, and foreign investment goals. The meeting placed renewed focus on execution milestones as the consortium continued work on the corridor.

Bechtel also announced a leadership change in its Mining & Metals business. On April 3, 2026, the company said Lucy Martin would become president of the division effective April 6, 2026, succeeding Ailie MacAdam, who was set to retire in May after a 41-year career. Craig Albert, Bechtel president and COO, described Martin as "a proven leader" with strategic vision, sector knowledge, operational discipline, and customer focus. Martin, who had spent more than two decades at Bechtel, said the division would focus on cost-effective development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes. The transition followed a period in which the Mining & Metals business recorded its highest new work bookings in 15 years during 2025 under MacAdam's leadership.

A separate Bechtel-linked development emerged from the education sector on April 7, 2026, when UNC Charlotte announced a leadership gift from alumni Craig and Darla Albert to launch The Albert School of Construction in the William States Lee College of Engineering. Craig Albert, who serves as president and chief operating officer of Bechtel Group, said there is a real shortage of construction capacity and that the program is designed to help meet workforce needs. The new school is intended to focus on the full capital project lifecycle, integrating engineering, business, and data science. Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber said the investment would align education more directly with regional and national needs, while Craig Albert said he hoped graduates would "climb the learning curve more quickly" and be equipped for the requirements of the industry.

Fluor's recent developments reflected momentum in digital infrastructure and continued investor attention to backlog quality and capital allocation. On April 22, 2026, Fluor Corporation announced it had been selected by TeraWulf to construct a 480-megawatt artificial intelligence data center in Kentucky, a project estimated at between $3 billion and $4 billion. Although financial details of Fluor's contract were not disclosed, the award added to its portfolio of complex engineering and construction projects and reinforced its role in data center delivery. The company's urban solutions segment, which includes data center work, contributed more than 59% of total revenue in 2025, underlining the strategic weight of that market. CEO Jim Breuer said Fluor was also in negotiations for additional major data center opportunities in the United States and Europe.

Another Fluor-related report focused on the company's strategic repositioning and market interest from prominent investor David Einhorn. Fluor first appeared in Einhorn's 13F portfolio in the second quarter of 2025 with 3.8 million shares, rising by more than 55% to 5.5 million shares in the third quarter of 2025 and reaching 5.56 million shares by the fourth quarter of 2025. Fluor's shift away from higher-risk fixed-price work was cited as a central factor. As of early 2026, approximately 82% of its $25.5 billion backlog was under reimbursable terms, a structure that passes much of inflation and cost-overrun exposure to clients. The company also repurchased $754 million in shares during 2025 and announced plans for an additional $1.4 billion in buybacks during 2026, supported in part by monetization of its NuScale Power stake, which generated $1.35 billion in the first quarter of 2026.

A further Fluor update on April 16, 2026, examined the composition of that $25.5 billion backlog against 2025 revenue of $15.5 billion. New awards totaled $12.0 billion in 2025, and 81% of year-end backlog plus 87% of new awards were reimbursable. Urban Solutions generated $9.2 billion in revenue in 2025, compared with $3.6 billion for Energy Solutions and $2.7 billion for Mission Solutions. On profitability, Fluor reported a GAAP net loss of $51 million for 2025, equal to a diluted loss per share of $0.31, while adjusted EBITDA was $504 million and adjusted diluted earnings per share were $2.19. The figures highlighted a contrast between headline GAAP performance and adjusted operating metrics, with backlog quality and project mix remaining central to assessments of execution visibility.

KBR's latest announcements pointed to expanding work in defense analytics, transportation modernization, and applied AI. On April 29, 2026, KBR said its Mission Technology Solutions division had won a $510 million contract from the Office of the Secretary of War, Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation Joint Data Support Division. The Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity multi-award contract covers joint data and analytic support services using a mix of firm-fixed-price, labor-hour, and time-and-materials structures. Work is expected to be performed mainly at the Pentagon and in Alexandria, Virginia, over five years. KBR said the work would involve collecting and organizing data on military equipment, capabilities, force structure, and global deployments, then analyzing it to support senior decision-makers. Stuart Bradie, KBR's president and CEO, said the company is focused on equipping the department with "mission-critical insights" to support timely and informed decisions.

Two days earlier, on April 27, 2026, KBR announced a separate recompete contract worth up to $200 million with the Department of Transportation's Volpe National Transportation Systems Center. The five-year Transportation Technology and Engineering Management Services agreement is a single-award blanket purchase agreement issued through the General Services Administration. KBR said it would provide engineering services that include artificial intelligence, machine learning, modeling and simulation, data analytics, and IT support from Cambridge, Massachusetts, and other global sites. The contract supports agencies including the Federal Aviation Administration, National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, and Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, linking KBR's technical services to transportation safety and network modernization priorities.

KBR also disclosed on April 27, 2026, that it had made a strategic investment in Applied Computing in the UK to deepen its AI capabilities across energy, chemicals, and industrial markets. The move represented the company's first direct investment in AI technology and included a multi-year joint development agreement to create exclusive AI products for the energy sector. KBR said it would combine Applied Computing's Orbital foundational model with its own licensed process technologies and sector expertise to produce tools spanning asset operations, capital projects, and next-generation technology de-risking. Greg Conlon, KBR's Chief Digital and Development Officer, said the investment would help create "a new paradigm for OpEx analytics and NextGen CapEx delivery," while Applied Computing CEO Callum Adamson pointed to KBR's global reach and industry depth as accelerants for commercial deployment.

AECOM's reported developments covered environmental treatment pilots, airport expansion, and urban reconnection planning. On April 28, 2026, AECOM said it had been selected by the Massachusetts Department of Environmental Protection to deliver a pilot program aimed at reducing and destroying PFAS at municipal wastewater treatment plants across the state. Working with CDM Smith, AECOM is set to execute 18 pilot tests across multiple materials and technologies in a compressed timeframe. The program is intended to evaluate PFAS separation and destruction approaches across liquid, solid, and air process streams. Sam Donelson, chief executive of AECOM's U.S. East and Latin America region, said the initiative reflects "a highly innovative approach" by testing multiple technologies in real-world conditions and across several treatment streams. The program is expected to generate data on performance, operational feasibility, and applicability for utilities dealing with rising PFAS scrutiny.

On the same date, April 28, 2026, vertical construction began on the New Concourse D at Chicago O'Hare International Airport, with the City of Chicago and AECOM Hunt Clayco Bowa advancing a 580,000-square-foot concourse under the broader ORDNext modernization program. The project is valued at $1.45 billion and is expected to be completed by late 2028. Officials said underground work had been completed and roughly 35% of foundational work finalized, including more than 90% of deep foundation caissons. Plans call for installation of a 195-foot tower crane to support above-ground work on the 73-foot-tall facility. The concourse is designed with 19 gates for narrow-body aircraft, with flexibility to convert up to nine gates for wide-body use, plus more than 20,000 square feet of lounge space, 30,000 square feet of retail and dining, and a children's play area. The Chicago Department of Aviation also reported that more than $830 million had been allocated to certified minority- and women-owned businesses in its capital program, equal to a 36.2% participation rate.

AECOM was also tied to Detroit's I-75 Cap Feasibility Study, an initiative intended to reconnect neighborhoods historically divided by the interstate. On April 24, 2026, the Downtown Detroit Partnership, together with the Michigan Department of Transportation and the City of Detroit, selected a lead firm for the work. Funded by a $2 million grant from the U.S. Department of Transportation's Neighborhood Access and Equity Program, the study is examining public park caps above I-75 near Brush Street, Woodward Avenue, and W. Grand River Avenue. The work runs from August 2025 through August 2026 and is expected to produce conceptual designs, cost estimates, and recommendations on phasing and long-term operations. The project is positioned around mobility, environmental benefit, civic space creation, and community engagement, with the study team expected to work with residents and other stakeholders throughout the planning process.

Across this peer group, the reported items from 2025 and 2026 show Turner Industries Group moving through geographic expansion and executive recognition, Bechtel managing leadership transition and infrastructure accountability, Fluor deepening its role in data-center construction while emphasizing reimbursable backlog and buybacks, KBR widening its AI and analytics footprint through government and industrial agreements, and AECOM advancing major environmental and infrastructure programs with measurable deployment details and public-sector partnerships.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-B8W3-SBHM-J491-00000-00Research and Markets Issues Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 2026 - $37.67 Billion Market Opportunities2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:05ZLexisNexisResearch and Markets Issues Report: Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Research 2026 - $37.67 Billion Market Opportunities

The "Neodymium Iron Boron (NdFeB) Permanent Magnets Market Report 2026" has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offering.

According to a release, the neodymium iron boron (NdFeB) permanent magnets market has experienced robust growth recently, with a projected expansion from $26.45 billion in 2025 to $28.34 billion by 2026, registering a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.1 percent. This growth trajectory can be linked to consumer electronics' miniaturization, increased demand for high-efficiency industrial motors, expansion of renewable energy sources like wind turbines, and a strong dependence on rare earth supply chains.

Forecasts indicate the NdFeB permanent magnets market will continue its upward climb, reaching $37.67 billion in 2030 with a CAGR of 7.4 percent. Factors propelling this growth include rising demand for electric vehicle traction motors, greater investment in advanced manufacturing methods, the need for lightweight magnetic components, expanding applications in medical device miniaturization, and a push towards recycling and sustainable rare earth sourcing. Emerging trends include a surge in demand for high-performance miniaturized magnets, development of high-temperature grade NdFeB magnets, adoption of corrosion-resistant coatings, and the utilization of 3D-printed magnet geometries.

The burgeoning demand for electric vehicles (EVs) is a significant driver for the NdFeB magnets market. EVs, reliant on high-performance permanent magnets like NdFeB for efficient motors, are on the rise due to stricter emission norms and a global shift towards clean mobility solutions. According to Kelley Blue Book, 2023 saw a record 1.2 million U.S. car buyers opting for EVs, representing 7.6 percent of the market, up from 5.9 percent in 2022.

To bolster supply chain resilience amid rising demand, companies in NdFeB magnets are expanding manufacturing capacities outside China. Initiatives aim to enhance production through new facilities and equipment upgrades. For instance, JS Link announced in 2025 a new production facility in Georgia, USA, expected to produce three kilotons of NdFeB magnets annually, with operations set for 2027, boosting non-Chinese supply capacity.

In May 2023, Neo Performance Materials acquired SG Technologies Group for $13.4 million, enhancing its NdFeB manufacturing capabilities in Europe. This move aims to support demand from sectors like electric vehicles, electronics, and clean energy. Key players in the market include TDK Corp., Daido Steel, Neo Performance Materials, and more.

NdFeB permanent magnets are essential for their strong magnetic performance in compact applications. They are available in forms including bonded, sintered, and 3D-printed magnets, suited to various industry needs from automotive to medical devices. In essence, the NdFeB permanent magnets market is poised for continued growth, driven by technological advancements and increasing demand from electric mobility and sustainable energy sectors.
